Am 16. April bin ich selbst in Farchant-: On 16 April, I made an off-field landing in Farchant-Nord myself. The meadow is flat and wide enough even for a DG1000. Due to the valley wind, a northbound landing is advisable. Contrary to previous comments, I wouldn’t recommend overrunning any of the cross-runways. The embankments are steep enough to send an aeroplane back into the air even at around 60 km/h. If you get too far, it’s better to deploy the air brakes and fly over the second cross-path. After getting out, I noticed the iron bars used to cordon off the western part of the field. They aren’t visible from the air. If you’re aware of this and aim for the eastern part along the road, it’s no problem. The remaining 35 metres in width are sufficient. A lovely field – I can highly recommend it. It looks even better in real life than the photos suggest.
Am 21.07.2022 dort gelandet. Landerichtu: Landed there on 21 July 2022. Landing heading 18. Very easy to spot thanks to the wind turbines. Approach without any obstacles and a smooth landing. Several options. My field with the wind turbines was to the east of the small road.
Am westlichen Rand ist ein Bach (Wasserg: On the western edge there is a stream (ditch), which is clearly visible despite the vegetation on the banks; the terrain slopes from east to west (approx. 5%). When approaching from direction 19, there is a power line, but it should be far enough away. At the northern end, there is a distinct mound of earth (see photos). The western field, which borders the north-south road, is relatively bumpy and there is a power line running directly over it! The northern fields are relatively steep, strewn with stones and likely to have animals on them. In my opinion, this is the only field or meadow suitable for landing amongst those available there. I landed there on 25 August 2020, heading 19; the landing and roll-out went smoothly. The stream made packing up a bit of a challenge (see photo), but it was manageable, and the local farmer’s staff were very helpful.

Anflug über den See 16, steigt leicht na: Approach over Lake 16, climbing slightly towards the south. Touch down only after the crossroads opposite the house. As of May 2018
Angeschaut am 29.06.2023. Der nördliche: Viewed on 29 June 2023. The northern part is overgrown, whilst in the south, on the field where you’d normally be able to land, there are currently bales of straw. So, not great:-(

Auf angegebenen (schmalen) Landefeld nur: On the specified (narrow) landing strip, there is only one marker post, approximately 100 metres from the western end. However, as a cluster of buildings would have to be flown over beforehand anyway, a landing could be made immediately after the marker post. Remaining length: approximately 300–400 metres
Auf dem Modellflugplatz ist nun auch noc: A safety fence (6–8 metres high) has now also been erected at the model aerodrome, covering approximately one-third of its width. Lightning conductors at least 12 metres high have now also been installed above the fuel tanks. In my personal planning, I have today downgraded the model aerodrome from Class C to Class D.
Auf dem Satellitenbild ist lediglich die: The satellite image shows only the (preferred) approach variant, which is across the valley. Whilst the description is generally correct, I would prefer to specify an approach towards 31 (so as not to contradict Figures 5 and 6). When approaching from direction 31, the meadow is on the left, but this is the only way the stated runway length of 250 metres is realistic; when approaching from direction 36, it is at most 170 metres. The considerable gradient of approximately 6 per cent should also be noted. The entry dated 7 March 2021 is out of date. These residential buildings are workers’ accommodation from the former Gotthard Tunnel construction site, which was dismantled long ago. These are no longer visible on the satellite image used by streckenflug.at, nor in Ernst Will’s approach video dated 5 April 2020. I would delete image 1 (as it also contradicts image 4) and replace images 5 and 6 with images that do not show the demolished houses (see also the appendix). For images 1 to 4, it would be helpful to include a note indicating the direction in which the photograph was taken – towards 09 or 27 (i.e. perpendicular to the 31 approach).
Aufsetzen erst nach Querweg empfehlenswe: It is advisable to land only after the cross-runway; take care of cables, trees and hedges on approach.
August 2020 >2m hoher Mais und Bewässeru: August 2020 > Maize over 2 metres tall and irrigation systems installed, making the land unworkable in summer!
August 2020 besucht, alles ok. Kein Wind: Visited in August 2020; everything was fine. No windsock.
Aussenlandefeld aus Katalog von Dittinge: Outlying airfield from the 2008 Dittingen catalogue.
Aussenlandung vom 27. Mai 2026. Auf dem: Off-field landing on 27 May 2026. Very tall vegetation (waist-high grass) across the entire site. Several concrete posts mark the boundaries. These are practically invisible when the vegetation is high. However, there are large gaps between the posts. There is enough space even for large aircraft. Please note: The entire site slopes gently down towards the lake in a south-easterly direction. See the yellow hatching. At the position of the aircraft shown on the map, the lateral slope is at its steepest – a proper hollow. That is exactly where I came to a halt with my 15-metre wing. See photos. The first choice is the area east of the posts. It is level here and can be approached from both directions. The second choice is the area between the middle and eastern rows of posts. Here, you should keep closer to the eastern side, as there is less lateral slope here and you can come to a halt south of the hollow. The third choice is an approach from the south-east towards the north-west. Slightly uphill, but no lateral slope. Probably the best choice in strong winds, but also the greatest danger due to the posts. Wind: In the event of an early landing (before 13:30), thermal winds are negligible. Therefore, an approach across the lake towards the north is recommended due to the rising terrain. The later in the afternoon and the more thermally active the air mass, the stronger the wind from the west/south-west becomes. In this case, consider the approach via the motorway towards the south or the third option. Conclusion: A very large hay meadow. No cows, but tall grass is to be expected in May. The posts are a nuisance; they are barely visible when the vegetation is high but can be managed, as they are static boundary markers. The position of the posts can be estimated. You can make out the boundary line by the vegetation. Furthermore, the large tree to the east of the fields and the embankment on the lakeshore serve as landmarks to help estimate the position. Ground conditions are excellent. No ditches.
Bei der Begehung im März 2026 wurde ein: During the site inspection in March 2026, a hole 100 metres long and approximately 3 metres deep was discovered in the centre of the meadow, dividing it into two separate areas. In addition, at the northern end, approximately 200 metres of the meadow has been excavated at a gentle gradient as part of the river restoration work. To the south, the meadow is bordered by the embankment of the new cycle path; to the north, it drops steeply down to the river. This leaves only two flat areas, each approximately 150 metres long and no more than 30 metres wide, which is why this meadow should only be used in the very worst-case scenario. The approach from the north-east is free of obstacles and is preferable, partly due to the gently sloping edge of the excavation – however, should the pilot overshoot the end of the first meadow, the aircraft would slide into the 3-metre-deep hole. Aerial photograph: Simeon Schmauß; ground photographs: Michael Watzke
Bei der CIM 2021 auf dem Hochplateau gel: Landed on the high plateau at CIM 2021.
Bei einer Besichtigung erschien das Feld: During a site visit, the field itself appeared to pose no problems; however, given its location in a fairly narrow valley, landing is likely to prove challenging due to the strong Maloja wind blowing from here into the Upper Engadine. Valley wind (on thermal days with no wider regional wind) from the south!
Bei meiner Besichtigung am 29.7.2022 ers: During my visit on 29 July 2022, the meadow to the south, running parallel to the row of trees, seemed to me to be a better place to land. There was no sign of the fence (note from 2021).
Beide Felder sind in der nördlichen Hälf: Both fields are quite undulating in the northern half, so the preferred landing direction is north
Beide Felder waren 2. Hälfte Juni nicht: Neither field had been mown in the second half of June. Both meadows are littered with posts, which are not visible when the meadow has not been mown. Based on a previous inspection, I would choose the northernmost field and land from the north, following the footpath towards the Mottolino valley station. The meadow near the snow depot is certainly suitable for landing, but damage to the aircraft must be accepted.
Beim Landefeld FISCHHAUSEN (DE), südlich: Unfortunately, there has recently been a change to the permanent horse fence at the FISCHHAUSEN (DE) landing field, south of Schliersee!
Bereits gemeldete Wiese ok!. Aktuelll is: The meadow already reported is fine! At present, the additional meadow shown on the map is longer and flat. As things stand, there are uniform mowing tracks across the entire meadow.
Besichtigt März 2024, unverändert gut: Inspected in March 2024; still in good condition
Besichtigt am 06.12.2021. Landeplatz für: Visited on 6 December 2021. Landing site for paragliders and hang-gliders.
Besichtigt am 19.5.2024 bei Regen. Nach: Inspected on 19 May 2024 in the rain. Rising towards the west (direction of the photo) by approx. 10 m across the length of the meadow (approx. 4%). Land in direction 24. Diagonally NE → SW or at the right-hand edge of the meadow (otherwise a slight lateral slope). Slight undulations across the landing direction. At the time of the site visit, there were no fences within the meadow, but there were some at the roadside. The presence of individual posts cannot be ruled out. There are alternatives in the immediate vicinity, but I could not find a better one. The terrain is generally very hilly.
Besichtigt am 20.11.2021. Spuren von Wei: Visited on 20 November 2021. Traces of grazing livestock, but apart from that, a lovely meadow.
Besichtigung 07.04.2019, UL-Piste, Grave: Site visit 7 April 2019, UL runway, gravel, approx. 300 metres long
Besichtigung 21.4.2022. Bewuchs ca. 20cm: Inspection on 21 April 2022. Vegetation approx. 20 cm
Besichtigung 21.4.2022. Bewuchs ca. 30cm: Inspection on 21 April 2022. Vegetation approx. 30 cm
Besichtigung 23.4.2022:. Die Bewässerung: Site visit 23 April 2022: The irrigation system is currently in place. Iron poles approximately 4 cm thick and 90 cm high. You should be able to see them during the short final approach in order to land within the clear strips. Once the system has been dismantled, we will have a first-class landing area 350 m long, level and completely smooth. See the satellite image below for the location of the sprinklers. There is a wall running alongside the road, and the first row of sprinklers (red dots) runs parallel to it, approximately 2 m away. There are then clear strips, each approximately 31 m wide, leading up to the next row of sprinklers.
Besichtigung 30.03.2022. Der Geröllhaufe: Site visit 30 March 2022. The pile of rubble at the eastern end of the airfield is approximately 10 metres high. This means that when approaching from the west, the airfield becomes very short. Picture 1 shows the position of the top of the pile of rubble. Picture 2 shows the pile of rubble photographed from the east. Approach from the west only in strong breezes; otherwise, land from the lake heading east.
Besichtigung am 01.06.2024. Wiese unverä: Inspection on 1 June 2024. The meadow remains in very good condition. Inspection video:
Besichtigung am 02.04.2025. Unverändert: Inspection on 2 April 2025. Still in very good condition. Perfectly level, firm ground. From the air, the area looks patchy and uneven, but this is not due to unevenness; it is simply the varying shades of the grass cover. Signposted as ‘Aire d’Atterisage’, i.e. a landing site – whether for paragliders/hang gliders or microlights is not specified. Inspection video:
Besichtigung am 02.06.2025. Feld unverän: Site visit on 2 June 2025. Field unchanged as described. Approach best from east to west, against the valley breeze blowing in from the west. Length not particularly generous: 250 metres, running parallel to the field boundaries as described. Can be extended to 350 metres if the diagonal of the area marked in blue is fully utilised. Low vegetation (clover??) in early June 2025. Site inspection video:
Besichtigung am 02.06.2025. Unveränderte: Site visit on 2 June 2025. Factors remaining unchanged that support a Category A classification: - The layout of the field remains unchanged. - More than adequate length. - Very level ground. - No gradient whatsoever. - Sufficient clear approach from east to west. - Approach is therefore against the valley breeze blowing from the west. A new, but unfortunately only temporary, factor in favour of Category A: - At the moment (early June 2025), the field is covered in recently harvested grain! Brilliant! A factor in favour of Category B: - It is unclear what will be grown there next. Will it be ploughed up? Will maize be planted again? Site inspection video:
Besichtigung am 02.09.2024. Etwa quadrat: Site visit on 2 September 2024. A roughly square paragliding landing area in the east-southeastern part. Also used occasionally as sheep pasture. Fencing is therefore possible, particularly in the west-north-western, elongated section. Caution: a large number of paragliders land here! Therefore, only Category C. The La Mure microlight airfield, just 3 km further north-north-east, is a better option. Site visit video:
Besichtigung am 02.09.2024. Feld unverän: Inspection on 2 September 2024. Field condition remains very good. Inspection video:
Inspection on 2 September 2024. Field unchanged, as described. Inspection video:
Besichtigung am 02.09.2024. La Mure ist: Site visit on 2 September 2024. La Mure is now a microlight airfield in very good condition. Definitely grade A. Video of the site visit:
Besichtigung am 02.09.2024. Wiese unverä: Site visit on 2 September 2024. The meadow remains in very good condition. The owner and the tenant are noticeably and understandably annoyed by the numerous paraglider landings (and perhaps even landings by microlights?!) During our conversation, we tried to explain the difference between such landings ‘for fun’ and our forced landings, which, of course, nobody does for fun. The owner urged us to include a note here stating that she and her tenant do not want any landings. She also asks that people refrain from frequent visits to her property, as it has apparently already happened that cars have turned round in the tall grass or even driven into the meadow just to have a look! Video of the site visit:
Besichtigung am 04.09.2021. Der Containe: Site visit on 4 September 2021. The container, which was still standing in the middle of the field in Roland Bieri’s photographs, was no longer there on 4 September 2021. In my opinion, the approach from the north is probably only possible BELOW the high-voltage power line; this would make the field very difficult to access. Windsock by the roadside.
Besichtigung am 04.09.2021. Ebener Berei: Site visit on 4 September 2021. A flat area approximately 900 m long, unfortunately very fragmented and predominantly divided by transverse features. Power lines along the western edge of the field. The northernmost part (east of the power line) is still the best. In the southern part, there are many transverse ditches running along the numerous field boundaries. The tarmac access road roughly in the middle of the area is, or was, a loading point for the air transport of high-voltage pylons. Mont Dauphin-St. Crépin airfield is only 5 km away; therefore, this is more of an emergency landing site. Video tour (better quality) of the northern part: Video tour (poorer quality) of the southern part:
Besichtigung am 04.09.2021. Leicht anste: Site visit on 4 September 2021. Slightly uphill from west to east. New pile of gravel on the approach from east to west (landing direction 24). Valley wind (on days with consistent thermals and no significant cross-regional wind) from the west. Site inspection video:
Besichtigung am 04.09.2021. Wiese ist eb: Site visit on 4 September 2021. The meadow is flat and can be landed on from both directions. There is a ditch running alongside the western row of bushes; between the end of the row of bushes in the west and the windsock in the east, there is a 50-metre-wide gap. There is no ditch there, but rather an underground drainage pipe; it is uneven but can be driven over. For both landing directions, it is best to treat the drainage pipe as a threshold and touch down just beyond it. Valley wind (on days with consistent thermals and no penetrating cross-regional wind) from the south. Site inspection video:
Besichtigung am 04.09.2021. Wiese steigt: Site visit on 4 September 2021. The meadow rises steeply from south to north. Landing is only possible from south to north. A power line crosses the second half of the meadow diagonally from south-east to north-west, recognisable by three pylons in the meadow. Touch down after the first cross-path, but before the power line, and roll out from underneath it. Site inspection video:
Besichtigung am 06.04.2023. Wiese unverä: Inspection on 6 April 2023. The meadow remains in very good condition. New inspection video:
Besichtigung am 06.09.2022. Wiese mit ni: Site visit on 6 September 2022. Meadow with low vegetation, but with uneven ground of approx. 10–20 cm on the south side. The northern side of the meadow is significantly better, as it is both wider and flatter. Landing is no problem, even with large aircraft. An approach from the north is recommended, as there are no trees in the approach path and the meadow is better there. The wind appears to be channelled and was significantly stronger than in the surrounding area. (Mostly southerly wind)
Besichtigung am 07.05.2025. Dieses Jahr: Site visit on 7 May 2025. This year, a legume (?) is growing in the field, which was already 1.5 metres (!!) tall in May. An update will follow once the crop has been cleared. Until then, we’ll unfortunately have to downgrade this otherwise excellent field to grade B. Site inspection video:
Besichtigung am 09.09.2021. Zwei Wiesen:: Site visit on 9 September 2021. Two fields: 1. Field north of the motorway: quite good. Very firm, level ground. When approaching from west to east (runway 07), a high-voltage power line runs diagonally across the approach path, but can be flown over. 2. Meadow south of the motorway: Caution! Two parallel, high-voltage power lines run directly along the western edge of the field, which are likely to make an approach from west to east impossible. The field south of the motorway is therefore likely only suitable for landing from east to west (landing direction 25). Valley wind (on days with consistent thermals and no strong, large-scale winds) from the east. Site inspection video:
Besichtigung am 12.03.2025. Belag: Schot: Inspection on 12 March 2025. Surface: gravel track. Conditions unchanged, with piles of stones to the east
Besichtigung am 12.03.2025. Feld unverän: Inspection on 12 March 2025. Field condition remains good.
Inspection on 12 March 2025. Field condition remains very good.
Besichtigung am 12.03.2025. Oberfläche G: Site visit on 12 March 2025. Surface: grass (not gravel; this is easily confused with the track slightly to the north). Field unchanged
Besichtigung am 13.11.1023. Nur 2 km öst: Site visit on 13 November 1023. Just 2 km east of Ilanz. Possibly an alternative to Ilanz. The large area, totalling over 700 metres in length, is divided into individual plots, unfortunately at right angles to the direction of the land; this could potentially be problematic depending on land use and the time of year. Partly arable farming. In the eastern third of the area, two strips of field run parallel to the valley, slightly elevated on a terrace; an overhead power line, which can be driven under, crosses in a north-south direction. Site visit video:
Besichtigung am 13.11.2023. Unverändert: Inspection on 13 November 2023. Still in good condition. Inspection video:

Site visit on 13 November 2023. Still in good condition. Unfortunately, the large area has been divided into individual plots at right angles to the direction of the land; depending on how the fields are used and the time of year, this could potentially be problematic. Video of the site visit:
Besichtigung am 14.03.2023. Die Felder b: Site visit on 14 March 2023. The fields near Montmaur are listed as Field No. 311 on page 136 of the official outfield catalogue “Guide des aires de sécurité dans les Alpes, Édition 3”. Two areas are marked there; the field described here, “Montmaur Nord”, is the northern and slightly poorer of these two areas. It slopes upwards from south to north, so landing from south to north is preferable. Take care when flying low over the river and road from the south: a low-hanging power line with concealed pylons crosses the southern, pointed end of the meadow in a roughly west-east direction. Site inspection videos: Part 1: Part 2:
Besichtigung am 14.03.2023. Erstaunlich: Site visit on 14 March 2023. An astonishingly good meadow! Flat and long. Both landing directions are possible. When approaching from east to west, there is a single tree. The approach can be made either through narrow gaps to the left or right between this single tree and the adjacent rows of trees, or over the single tree – the field is long enough for this too. An approach from west to east is also possible and free of obstacles. Inspection video:
Besichtigung am 14.03.2023. Exzellente F: Site visit on 14 March 2023. Excellent fields. Slightly sloping from south to north. In calm conditions, landing is therefore from south to north. When there is a southerly wind, it is also possible to land from north to south. The very longest field lies directly to the east, parallel to the road, but the longest of the fields to the west of the road is also very good. Do not drive over the field boundaries (=colour boundaries). Irrigation systems are laid underground, but the individual sprinklers do not appear to be permanently in place; in any case, there were none there in mid-March. Site visit video:
Besichtigung am 14.03.2023. Unverändert: Site visit on 14 March 2023. Still in very good condition. The meadows shown here are the north-western area ‘1’ of the two areas marked in the French ‘Papier-Außenlande’ catalogue. Area “2”, situated further to the south-east, is also in very good condition. Inspection video for Area 1: Inspection video for Area 2 (further to the south-east):
Besichtigung am 14.03.2023. Unverändert:: Site visit on 14 March 2023. No changes:. Pros: The runway itself is fine – flat and long – and there is a windsock. Cons: The airfield is situated on a plateau. Access is via a gravel track in poor condition. Conclusion: The numerous large meadows and fields below the plateau in the valley are much better! Video of the visit:
Besichtigung am 14.11.2023. In einem Sei: Site visit on 14 November 2023. In a side valley of the Vorderrhein Valley. Approx. 8 km SSW of Ilanz. Approx. 900 m above sea level, making it 200 m higher than Ilanz. Possibly an alternative to Ilanz? A large, flat area, approx. 600 m long, with several fields. Unfortunately, the fields are laid out at right angles to the direction of flight. A prominent, straight farm track. The fields to the north-west of this track are less undulating. Site visit video:
Besichtigung am 18.03.2022. Mehrere Feld: Visited on 18 March 2022. Several fields. Lots of small electricity or telephone lines running alongside the farm tracks and roads. Nevertheless, a good option that deserves an ‘A’ rather than a ‘B’. Site visit video: Listed as site no. 132 on page 104 in the French rural area catalogue ‘Guide des aires de sécurité dans les Alpes, Édition 3’.
Besichtigung am 18.03.2022. Unverändert: Inspection on 18 March 2022. Still in good condition. Do not drive over the field boundaries (identifiable by the difference in colour). Listed as Field No. 131, Variant 2, on pages 102–103 in the French off-road catalogue ‘Guide des aires de sécurité dans les Alpes, Édition 3’.

Visit on 18 March 2022. Still very good. The field slopes upwards from south to north; therefore, the landing direction should be from south to north where possible. Inspection video: Listed as field no. 130 on pages 100–101 in the French off-airport landing catalogue ‘Guide des aires de sécurité dans les Alpes, Édition 3’.
Besichtigung am 21.12.2024. Eine von zah: Site visit on 21 December 2024. One of numerous excellent landing sites in the Westallgäu’s foothills of the Alps, where landing conditions are generally very good. The fact that this particular meadow has made it into the catalogue is likely to have historical reasons: from 1959 to 1982, this was the gliding site of the Westallgäu Air Sports Group from Lindenberg (source: ), and remained a popular meeting place for model aircraft enthusiasts for many years afterwards. In the 1990s and 2000s, gravel pits were dug into the large meadow. But even these have since been filled in, levelled and restored to meadows, so that by 2024 the meadow will certainly be better (!) than it was during the last inspection in 2003. In fact, an airfield could be set up here again. Tour video:
Besichtigung am 22.02.2025. Wiesen unver: Site visit on 22 February 2025. The meadows remained unchanged as described. A mobile pasture fence running east to west divided the meadows roughly in the middle of their length into two halves, as described by Christoph and Thomas. Many horses are grazing all around. The meadows themselves would be Category B; without the mobile pasture fence, they would even be a very good B. However, given the risk that horses might also be standing on the strip of land, Category C is appropriate! Inspection video:
Besichtigung am 22.03.2025. Feld unverän: Inspection on 22 March 2025. Field still in very good condition; windsock present
Besichtigung am 22.03.2026. Zustand unve: Inspection on 22 March 2026. Condition remains good.
Besichtigung am 23.04.2022. Merci Jean f: Visit on 23 April 2022. Thanks, Jean, for maintaining the Marcoux airstrip! If anyone has a windsock, Jean would be delighted to have a new one ;-). On the day of our visit, he was maintaining the runway and repairing some of the damage caused by meltwater. The runway is a bit narrower in the middle, as shown in the photos, but it should be fine.
Besichtigung am 24.03.2023. Feld unverän: Inspection on 24 March 2023. Field still in very good condition. Inspection video, part 1: Inspection video, part 2:

Inspection on 24 March 2023. Field unchanged. In spring, whilst the maize is still low and the irrigation pipes have not yet been laid, this is a very good, A-grade field. Later in the year, however, it is inaccessible due to the maize and irrigation pipes – see Christian Heiting’s note! New viewing video:
Besichtigung am 24.03.2023. UL-Piste mit: Site visit on 24 March 2023. UL runway with a windsock. The wind direction increases from south-east to north-west; therefore, landings are only permitted from south-east to north-west. Two white, cone-shaped landing markers indicate the threshold. Do not touch down before the threshold, as the ground in front of it is uneven. However, do not touch down too far beyond the threshold either, as the ground becomes increasingly uneven during a long roll-out uphill towards the north-west. The best section is the first 150 metres past the threshold. From the air, the field looks patchy in colour, uneven and therefore worse than it actually is. However, there are also many agricultural fields all around. It’s a difficult choice; with the UL runway, at least you know what you’re getting. In March 2023, the runway was rather poorly maintained, with a few small thorny bushes here and there. Reconnaissance video:
Besichtigung am 24.03.2023. Wiesenfläche: Site visit on 24 March 2023. The meadow is divided into two halves by a ditch. The eastern half is lower-lying and flat, whilst the western half rises steeply from the ditch towards the west. Approach from east to west (out of the valley): glide-in over the flat eastern half of the meadow. Touchdown after the ditch, roll-out uphill. Longest roll-out distance and steepest climb along the diagonal. With this approach direction, the meadow is a ‘good’ C-meadow or perhaps even a poor B. Approach from west to east (valley-inward): Glide in along the elongated farm buildings, as low as possible over the western half of the meadow, which slopes steeply in this approach direction from west to east. Touch down immediately after the ditch, roll-out on the flat and not very long eastern half. This approach direction is likely only feasible with a strong easterly wind blowing out of the valley. In this approach direction, the meadow is definitely only a C. Reconnaissance video part 1 (filmed from the western end):. Reconnaissance video part 2 (filmed from the eastern end):
Besichtigung am 24.04.2022. Kleiner Grab: Site visit on 24 April 2022. A small ditch on the eastern side of the field, between shrubs and trees. A deeper ditch on the western side. Approach clear and unobstructed.
Besichtigung am 24.04.2022. UL-Landeplat: Visited on 24 April 2022. UL landing site, viewed ‘only from a little further away’. Looks good. There are riders standing along the red line.
Besichtigung am 25.06.2024. Anflug aus b: Site visit on 25 June 2024. Approach possible from both directions. Minimal cross-slope from the road in the north down to the river in the south. The power line running alongside the road does not cause any problems. The field is quite stony, but apart from that it is Category A. On Google Maps, a transverse field boundary had previously been visible, dividing the area at its narrowest point into two roughly equal sections. This field boundary was definitely no longer present during the on-site inspection on 25 June 2024; the field has clearly been tilled and sown in one continuous stretch along its full length. If this remains the case in the long term, a very important new landing site has been created, making the route to Mont Ventoux significantly safer! Site visit video:
Besichtigung am 25.06.2024. Feld unverän: Inspection on 25 June 2024. Field still in good condition. The cereal was tall at the end of June; apart from that, it was in good condition. Inspection video:
Besichtigung am 25.06.2024. Südsüdöstlic: Site visit on 25 June 2024. South-southeasterly edge of the field with no gradient, though slightly marshy there. From there, heading north (across the field), the ground rises slightly. Heading towards the village (along the field), the gradient is only minimal. However, the village itself is situated significantly higher than the meadow. An approach is therefore likely only possible from east to west (towards the village). At the end of June 2024, the vegetation consisted of tall grass. The meadow is not (yet) included in the French outlying areas catalogue. Site visit video:
Besichtigung am 26.03.2023. Das längste,: Site visit on 26 March 2023. The longest, northernmost of the fields shown on the map has a (non-critical) power line running north-south along the farm track at the eastern edge of the field. Please note! A low-hanging cross-line branches off from this towards the west, crossing the field slightly south of its centre! Site visit video:
Besichtigung am 26.03.2023. Gelände stei: Site visit on 26 March 2023. The terrain rises significantly from south to north; there is also a row of trees along the northern edge of the field. Landing is therefore only recommended from south to north. Site visit video, part 1: Site visit video, part 2:
Besichtigung am 26.03.2023. Wiesen unver: Inspection on 26 March 2023. Meadows still in good condition. Inspection video, part 1: Inspection video, part 2:
Besichtigung am 30.03.2022. Das Feld ist: Site visit on 30 March 2022. The field is a pasture situated at an altitude of approximately 860. It is easily accessible by air from the lake and suitable for landing. Landing direction: NW. The field is enclosed by an electric pasture fence. It is therefore likely to be usable in spring; from May onwards, cows are to be expected on the meadow. The first 100 metres of the meadow are uneven and slope more steeply, so they cannot be used. However, this section is clearly distinguishable and marked by a transverse ridge (2nd picture) After that, approximately 500 m are available for landing (1st picture). In addition to this meadow, another meadow on the plateau further up to the right is also suitable for landing. However, to reach it, you must first fly over a 5-metre-high power line running across the area
Besichtigung am 31.03.2024, Landung am 0: Inspection on 31 March 2024, landing on 6 April 2024. Field still in very good condition. The field is planted with durum wheat (high in protein, says the friendly farmer!). Now in spring, whilst the crop is still low, it’s ideal for landing. Later in the year, the tall grain may become a problem. Inspection video:
Besichtigung am 31.03.2024. Dieses Feld: Site visit on 31 March 2024. This field is listed in the French outfield catalogue “Guide des aires de sécurité” as outfield No. 312 and is an alternative to the nearby “Valenty” cereal field. Generally speaking, the wide Durance Valley offers a great many flat areas in this region; however, some of these are unavailable all year round due to fruit cultivation, whilst others are unavailable depending on the season due to cereal cultivation. Unfortunately, it is unclear what is being grown on the field shown here. At the end of March, it looked like grass. Site visit video:
Besichtigung am 31.03.2024. Feld unverän: Inspection on 31 March 2024. Field condition remains good. Inspection video:
Inspection on 31 March 2024. The field remains in very good condition. Inspection video:
Besichtigung am 8. 4. 2023:. Ansteigend: Site visit on 8 April 2023: Sloping upwards from south to north. Landing is only practical from the south! Approach over a garden plot with tall vegetation. The field is relatively undulating and not particularly long. Given the approach over the obstacle, I would classify it as an emergency landing field.
Besichtigung am 8. 4. 2023:. Der UL-Plat: Site visit on 8 April 2023: The UL airstrip still appears to be ‘well-maintained’. Even at half its length (there was currently a snow groomer in the way), the flat section is still suitable for landing. It is certainly a good place to land.
Besichtigung aus der Luft. Vorsicht, auf: Aerial view. Please note: there are pedestrians visible on the dirt track.
Besichtigung durch Freunde am 29.03.2022: Viewing by friends on 29 March 2022. A good field at this time of year in March, when the vegetation is low. However, irrigation pipes were already in place. Heavy vegetation is possible in summer! Viewing video: The field near Espinasses is listed as field no. 317 on pages 142–143 in the French outlying areas catalogue “Guide des aires de sécurité dans les Alpes, Édition 3”.
Besichtigung im Juni 2018, relativ enges: Visited in June 2018; a relatively small field with a windsock (paragliding landing site)
Winter visit. Length approx. 300m. Approach on both sides over obstacles. Otherwise, a good landing area.
Besichtigung zu Fuß Anfang September 202: Walking tour in early September 2025. A meadow maintained for agricultural purposes. It can therefore be used all year round. However, the valley is quite narrow, which effectively rules out a cross-wind approach.
Besucht Ende August 2020, am Rand einzel: Visited at the end of August 2020; a few taller stems/bushes at the edge. No wind sock, but markings present.
Bewuchs noch tief. Ob das nördliche Feld: The vegetation is still quite dense. I doubt whether the northern field – with the very high power line running alongside the railway line on the approach – is accessible by air. However, there are several very good alternatives throughout the valley.
Bin am 07.08.25 drauf gelandet. Alles be: I ended up there on 7 August 25. Everything’s great!
Bin die Wiese schon aus beiden Richtunge: I’ve flown into the meadow from both directions before, although it was a while ago now.
Blick Richtung Norden im Juli 2021: View towards the north in July 2021
Das Aussenlandefeld liegt optimal in der: The outfield is ideally situated in the direction of the wind and can be approached without any problems in the event of valley winds or southerly winds. Please note that the fields are divided by hay posts! At this outfield, the high-voltage power lines have been consolidated to the east of the motorway.
Das Feld Ende April besucht. Bilder aus: Visited the field at the end of April. Close-up photos.
Das Feld ist quer zur Landerichtung gene: The field is sloped at an angle to the direction of approach (sloping down towards the motorway).
Das Feld ist sehr wellig und in Landeric: The field is very undulating and slopes noticeably in the direction of landing. It should only be used in an absolute emergency. The field in Schiers, just 5 km away, is more suitable.
Das Feld sieht gut aus. Landung Hangaufw: The field looks good. Landing uphill.
Das Feld westlich der Strasse nur im Not: Only use the field west of the road in an emergency. The area marked with a red cross is now a strawberry field and cannot be landed on. The area between the road and the strawberry field can be landed on, but it is narrow. There is also a portable toilet block and a stall at the southern end of the field. Landing is only permitted from the north. The area marked with an orange cross is only suitable in an emergency. This part of the meadow slopes upwards towards the western edge of the woodland. IT IS BETTER TO USE THE FIELD EAST OF THE ROAD – OR TO ALTERNATELY USE EBNAT-KAPPEL.
Das Feld östlich des Flusses ist eine gu: The field to the east of the river is a good landing spot in June 2025. It has been recently planted. The field to the west of the river is somewhat narrower, but is still suitable for landing. The western side is preferable.
Das Schmelzwasser des Flusses hat zwar a: Although the river’s meltwater has eroded the rock away by about 10 metres on either side at the halfway point, it is still possible to land open-class aircraft there without any problems. The area appears to have been prepared and fitted with landing markers this summer. If you’re unsure, it’s also fine to touch down at the halfway point (total length approx. 550 metres).
Das ganze Wiesen-Areal ist landetechnisc: The entire meadow area is defined, in terms of landing, by 2-metre-wide ditches (dotted lines)! You must position yourself precisely for landing in accordance with the marked fields! Landing area 1, right next to the road, is limited to a maximum width of 40 metres between the barn’s outline and the roadside trees, but it is the longest and best! The paths can be driven over! Landing field 2 has a 40-metre narrowing in the middle caused by the drainage ditch and a pile of wood (marked with an arrow)!
Das in der 1. Auflage markierten Feld ha: The field marked in the 1st edition has a solid barbed-wire fence running down the middle!!! The field to the north of it is landable, apart from a long, winding ridge running parallel to the fence. The ridge can be driven over. There is also a fence by the field to the south (100 metres further west)!
Das nördliche Alternativfeld ist ein Mai: The alternative field to the north is now a maize field and no longer a meadow! It can only be used until around the end of June! To the north of it lies an orchard! It is absolutely essential to be aware of the high-voltage power lines in the area around the Eisack River, particularly when approaching from south to north (04°)! A difficult landing site! Landing feels like landing in a saucepan! It’s better to use Sterzing or Obervintl!
Der Anflug 27 geht über die Wasserleitun: Approach 27 goes over the water main, whilst Approach 09 goes over the houses. There’s plenty of space, but you might be taken by surprise by a few wooden posts.
Der Endanflug von Norden (was bei Talwin: The final approach from the north (which is likely to be the norm in the case of valley winds) takes you over a high-voltage power line. The field is undulating and is crossed by two transverse ditches. It is possible to avoid the container in the middle of it.
Der Flugplatz Sallanches im Tal von Cham: Sallanches Airfield in the Chamonix valley was closed by a government decree on 1 September 2020. The buildings and hangar belonging to the microlight club have since been demolished, but the runway has not been physically destroyed, as it is to serve as a car park for the future development zone established by the Sallanches local council.
Der Teil des Wiesenareals, das von den G: The part of the meadow used by paragliders is very undulating and is not recommended.
Der UL-Platz wurde auf die Nordseite vom: The UL site has been moved to the north side of the river!
Der Zustand ist in Ordnung. Die Auswasch: The surface is in good condition. The erosion has been repaired and levelled out relatively recently. Nevertheless, this area should not be used for parking or manoeuvring.
Der in der Flugaufnahme noch sichtbare u: The mast storage area, which was still visible in the aerial photograph and documented in the 2021 images, has been cleared away, and the meadow – including the section along the riverside road – is once again usable (see Street View, June 2024)
Der südliche Teil und 1km nördlich der T: The southern section and the section on the embankment 1 km to the north, which has a steeper side slope. There were a few low posts in there that could cause damage, but you can get down there safely.
Der westliche Teil wird aktuell als Rind: The western part is currently used as cattle pasture.
Die Bearbeitungsrichtung der Wiese änder: The direction in which the meadow is being worked changes from time to time; at present (photo taken on 29 June 2022), it is tending towards 33.
Die Besichtigung war am 10.04.2019, ein: The site visit took place on 10 April 2019; the field was in relatively good condition. Other fields previously documented as potential landing sites are in significantly poorer condition.
Die Bezeichnung ist nicht korrekt. Der O: That name is incorrect. The village is called Jenaz. Furna is further up the hillside.
Die Felder an der Straße entlang sind nu: The fields along the road are only moderately suitable. There are lots of posts and a mobile chicken coop that keeps getting moved around. The fields to the west are much more suitable.
Die Hindernisse durch die Bauarbeiten si: The obstacles caused by the construction work have been removed. The landing areas are currently meadows, which are divided by temporary fences (two fences, dividing the meadow into three sections along the railway line).
Die Koordinaten dieses Platzes sind fals: The coordinates for this airfield are incorrect! The microlight airfield is 2.5 km to the east. Coordinates: N 47° 10' 38", E 6° 48.45". Runway length: 350 m. I did not visit the site.
Die Landerichtung 04 ist aus meiner Sich: In my view, the 04 landing direction is very challenging on the meadow shown on the map. There is a gradient of almost 10 metres. The meadow on the other side of the Inn, which used to be accessible from both directions, is no longer shown on the map.
Die Landewiese in Thusis ist übrigens am: Incidentally, the landing field in Thusis is no longer usable at the location shown on the map, as a motorway service station now stands there. Fields to the south and south-east of the former landing field are suitable alternatives; these can be used without any problems.
Die Wiese hat im kurzen Endteil 27 eine: There is a high-voltage power line in the short final section 27 of the airfield, so the approach should only be made to 09.
Die Wiese hinter dem Edeka (roter Pfeil): The field behind the Edeka (red arrow) is a good alternative. There are only a few gentle undulations. Land just past the crossroads (ditch – see picture, towards the Edeka). There is a post at the start of the field on the left-hand side (see picture).
Die Wiese ist kürzer geworden, der östli: The meadow has become smaller; the eastern part is now completely planted with fruit trees!
Die Wiese westlich davon ist zwar auch n: The meadow to the west of it isn’t level either, but depending on the current vegetation, I think it’s still more suitable. Please note: there is a cable in the southern part
Die Wiese wurde durch hydraulische Arbei: The meadow has been altered by hydraulic works and is no longer usable. The remaining 180-metre-long meadow to the south of the new lake is not recommended.
Die Wiese wurde erst im vergangenen Jahr: The meadow was only levelled and cleared of stones last year. Watch out for the ditch below; keep to the left-hand side of the road – there are small posts on the right.
Die Wiese wurde in ein Maisfeld verwande: The meadow has been turned into a maize field. All that’s left is a narrow strip of meadow near the stream!
Die Wiesen im NW vom Fluss gibt es nicht: The meadows to the north-west of the river no longer exist – or rather, they’re now a golf course!
Die Wiesen sind insgesamt ca. 350 m lang: The meadows are approximately 350 m long in total and flat. An approach from the west is only feasible in the event of a strong headwind and with very effective flaps, due to the high-voltage power line running down the valley side. The final section, at an altitude of at least 100 m immediately at the start of the meadow, requires an extremely steep approach! I would approach from the east; the valley is wide enough for a ‘southern circuit’ and you can touch down early enough to fly under the power line running across the meadow. The pylons are spaced approx. 40–50 m apart and are approx. 7–8 m high; the cables sag to a height of approx. 5 m. The touchdown point is level with the two huts; one of them has a new roof covering as of 2020 (Google Maps). The roll-out distance is also long enough for tailwind landings (which will probably be the case most of the time). In the southern third, there are plastic or reinforced concrete marker posts with white flags attached; therefore, the second pole spacing from the Vorderrhein would be the ideal line to ensure sufficient distance from the markers. I would definitely prefer these meadows to the landing fields near Disentis, even if landing between the poles does get the heart racing.
Die Wiesen von Male kannst du alle lösch: You can delete all the meadows in Male! They’re littered with wooden stamps. The potential meadow should be classified as B; information is located near the path.
Die alte Wiese ist nicht mehr landbar, d: The old meadow is no longer suitable for landing, as numerous irrigation pipes have been laid and a quarry has been established in the northern part.
Die einzige Wiese in dieser Gegend wurde: The only meadow in this area has just been ‘planted’ with fruit trees and is now being used for fruit cultivation. As far as I can see, there isn’t a single meadow in the Vinschgau Valley!
Die verbliebene Wiese hinter dem früher: The remaining meadow behind the horse fence described earlier was, today, 21 February 2021, divided in the middle by two portable pasture fences, making it very difficult to land and leaving only a very short landing area on either the front or back half.
Dieses Feld ist seit einigen Jahren wege: It has not been possible to land at this field for several years now due to construction work. This field has not been landable for a number of years now due to construction
Different directions possible. Slight wa: Different directions possible. Slight waves in the terrain. (Info from French catalogue: Hills along the final depending on landing direction. Owner possibly difficult.)
Difficult field, ONLY for emergency in c: Difficult field, ONLY for emergency in case Thorame Basse cannot be reached!

Difficult field, ONLY for emergency in case Thorame Basse cannot be reached! Landing direction north-west (uphill), left downwind (south), base near the river (south-east), very short final. Uneven terrain. Turbulences in north-west-wind conditions. Approach of car/Trailer from south-east. 360°-view here:. Photos Jörn Leiber in August 2020.
Ditch at the southern end, keep left to: Ditch at the southern end, keep left to the road, right side small stakes
Ditch in the middle of the field, land i: Ditch in the middle of the field, land in the second part!
Ditches at the beginning and end of fiel: Ditches at the beginning and end of field!
Dort gibt eine Landewiesen für Landung R: There is a grassy field suitable for landing at a heading of 50°, 173 metres long. The path at the end cannot be driven along in the section where it runs at a heading of 110°. Approach between the trees and touch down immediately after the trees. Aim for a spot closer to the track, as there is an irrigation system further away from it. Approach between the hill and the church. The field, which runs in a 50° direction, can be approached from a 230° direction.
Downwind on East Side. Landing towards n: Downwind on East Side. Landing towards north is preferred, on the east side of the ditch. Owner does not want landings just for fun (e.g. UL)! Access for cars rather on the northern part. 360°-view here:. Photos Jörn Leiber in August 2020.
Drei parallele Felder mit unterschiedlic: Three parallel fields with different types of vegetation (cereals, artificial grassland). Easily approachable from the west.
Ebene Wiese. Anflug in beide Richtungen: Flat meadow. Approaches are possible from both directions, although when approaching from direction 34, you fly very close to the power line. When approaching from direction 16, you can taxi across the path at the end, although this is probably not necessary. -- A much better option than the old, registered meadow next door. --
Eigentlich gute Wiese und eine der wenig: It’s actually a good meadow and one of the few heading south towards the Jauffen Pass. The irrigation systems were also an issue during the site visit on 24 September 2016. The distance would be suitable for at least 15m and 18m, but would probably not be visible if the vegetation were taller. The irrigation pipes are also visible on Street View!
Eine Kuhherde auf der Landewiese. Stand:: A herd of cows on the landing field. As at 25 August 2021
Eine weitere gute 400 m lange Wiese ist: There is another good 400-metre-long meadow 3 km to the south, to the west of the Eisack river, near Neustift Abbey! No power lines; approach only from south to north, on a slight incline!
One of the fields is planted with rapeseed. However, there are numerous large fields and meadows in the valley between Balsthal and Herbetswil that are well suited for farming.
Einfacher zu landen als Latterbach-Wimmi: Easier to land than Latterbach-Wimmis; no jet effect. Small hill (Pintel) on approach. Landing direction 320°. Approach over the north side of the Niesen, which often provides lift, unlike the jet effect at the Simmenflue.
Enges Tal, Begehung ist aus 2008! Somit: Narrow valley; the site visit was in 2008! Therefore, up-to-date information is required!
Ergänzend noch 2 Fotos. Daß Feld ist deu: Here are two more photos. The field is significantly longer than the stated 250 metres. The paragliders land on the section marked in red.
Ergänzend zwei Bilder vom alternativen F: In addition, here are two photos of the alternative field a few kilometres south of the gravel track along the Route Napoléon. It should be easy to land on. Please note: this is NOT the officially designated field.
Ergänzung zu Holgers Informationen:. - b: Additional information to Holger’s details: - Among UL pilots, the airstrip is known as “Cipières”. - There is a sort of ‘approach chart’ here: - The website of the local club mentioned in it does not appear to be online at present (25 February 2025).
Erst ab dem Weg querab des Almhofes aufs: Only start from the path running across from the Almhof. Running parallel to the path (photo 29 June 2022) is a low-voltage electric pasture fence.
Es handelt sich um den UL-Flugplatz Pamp: This is the Pampelonne microlight airfield, LF8357 (see also ). Christoph Dolainsky’s latest comment refers to Runway 12, which has a steep climb. Runway 30 is therefore not really suitable as it slopes downwards. As far as I recall, Runway 07 also slopes slightly uphill, whilst Runway 25 slopes slightly downhill. It is certainly suitable for a field landing. I wouldn’t attempt a self-launch there, though.
Es ist ein Außenstartgelände, auf dem me: It is an outdoor airfield where several motorised aircraft are stationed.
Es kann nur Piste 17 bergauf gelandet we: You can only land on Runway 17 heading uphill, as the field is on a slope and the approach from the other direction is obstructed by a large bush. Land right next to the road. An ‘H’ is marked almost at the end of the usable part of the field (see satellite image).
Es sind mehrere Wiesen in unmittelbarer: There are several meadows in the immediate vicinity where you can land. Unfortunately, the ground in some of the meadows is uneven, and the individual patches are generally very small and marked out by wooden posts. I would rate the category as somewhere between B and C.
Es sind zwei Wiesen durch ca. 1m hohen Z: There are two meadows separated by a fence approximately 1 metre high (difficult to see). The southern part was very easy to land on. The northern part was too short.
Eventuell gibt es aktuellere Info, aber: There may be more up-to-date information available, but given the fencing, the unpredictable wind conditions and the large number of walkers, I consider the landing area to be problematic.
Fallen teilweise in Landerichtung 28 auf: In some places, they drop by 5–8 metres to 250 metres in the 28 direction.
Fand im Juni 2012 hohes Gras vor, aber i: I found tall grass there in June 2012, but there are loads of posts in the meadows, which could lead to a nasty surprise if you land there. Watch out for the (well-known) high-voltage power line.
Feld fällt Richtung Autobahn ab, am best: The field slopes down towards the motorway; it’s best to land diagonally uphill.
Feld im August 2022 besucht. Neben dem m: Visited this field in August 2022. Apart from the marked section, there are a few stones here and there, but these can be driven over. Access from the east is possible. This is an important field if, when approaching from the south, you are unable to make the jump onto the plateau. Please note: The Riet catalogue listed a field called ‘Lapian’ 2.5 km east of Alfedana. Today, it is completely inaccessible!
Feld ist eben und bretthart. Nur kleiner: The field is flat and rock-hard. There are just a few small stones here and there. Easy to land on, but: be careful – there’s a ditch running through the southern part. Only use the northern half, along the path. Access to the field is at the northern tip!
Field has been surveyed few days ago, su: Field has been surveyed few days ago, surface is good, Power lines still present and hazardous.
Field is in good condition, all the surf: Field is in good condition, all the surface seems to be usable, two visible obstacles in the western part. Smooth even terrain with short grass. Easy access from the road. Grass may be high in late spring/summer. [ WeGlide Flug ]
Field path separates the two fields!: Field path separates the two fields!
Field slopes down towards the motorway: Field slopes down towards the motorway
Fluglager findet seit diesem Jahr wieder: The model aircraft airshow has been taking place again this year. Plans are already underway for 2025. The ‘Mitte’ runway is covered with metal honeycomb panels, making it clearly visible and easy to land on. Landings are also possible to the north and south, although the grass is a little taller depending on the season.
Flugplatz ist geschlossen - Passy ist Au: The airfield is closed – Passy is an alternative. Further information:
Former airfield, partly used as RV park,: Former airfield, partly used as RV park, pedestrians, helicopter landing pad in the middle.
Frisch gemäht. Entlang der Straße ist ei: Freshly mown. There is a fence running along the road. Use the southern part of the meadow
Fruehjahr 2004: Im Nord-Osten des Feldes: Spring 2004: In the north-east of the field, part of the land has been planted with an orchard. The trees are not very old, as the area was still just a meadow when it was inspected a few years ago. The windsock on the north-west side is still there this year.
Frühjahrs Segelfluglager finden nicht me: Spring gliding camps no longer take place. However, the old runway is still open and usable.
Fuer Termon UL muesst ihr einen neuen Fl: For Termon UL, you’ll need to add a new airfield – it’s located here: N46°16'16" E011°01'32", elevation 682m. Course 24, 300m. Land uphill only. Frequency 130.00(?)
Gelandet am 11.09.2025, vorher schon bes: Landed on 11 September 2025; had visited the site previously. Large meadow, slightly undulating with rising terrain. Approach from the north-west. Overall, very easy to land at. However, please note the following for the return journey: - It is impossible to drive from the N94 under the bridge and through the woods northwards to the meadow with a trailer (tight bends, road barely visible)! - You must approach from the north via the village of Pra Périer! [ WeGlide flight ]
Gelände hängt zu Straße hin. Anflug frei: The site slopes down towards the road. Clear approach. Not too long. You may need to approach diagonally from the road.
Genau 30m von der Halle zum Feldrand!: Exactly 30 metres from the hall to the edge of the field!
Generell gut, im Frühjahr je nach Regenf: Generally good, but in spring, depending on rainfall, the field may be very wet! (This is also the general advice from our French colleagues)
Glatte Wiese ohne Beregnungsanlage. Quer: A smooth meadow with no sprinkler system. Running across it slightly south of the centre, at a height of about 3–4 metres, is a thin wire (telephone?) suspended from two flimsy wooden slats. See satellite image with sketch. Landing from the south, approaching over the trees, is fairly straightforward. Short landing! Touch down before the wire! Landing from the north, approaching over Quellenhof with a very steep descent, is quite difficult if you want to touch down before the wire. In an emergency, the wire is likely to give way.
Golf course with 2m net in the approach,: Golf course with 2m net in the approach, partly very uneven
Good grass field for gliders, ultralight: Good grass field for gliders, ultralight and general aviation
Grand-Prix-Landefeld: Liegt zwischen Tie: Grand Prix landing field: Located between Tiefencastel and Alvaneu-Bad. The terrain probably slopes upwards towards the south-west. According to Tilo: it was a good, harvested, freshly ploughed and suitable stubble field.
Grand-Prix-Landefeld:Hier landeten 7 Gra: Grand Prix landing strip: Seven Grand Prix pilots landed here without sustaining any damage
Grosses Feld östlich der bereits eingetr: The large field to the east of the one already marked is suitable for landing in direction 26 and slopes gently upwards towards the woodland. Inspected on 12 February 2022
Guide des Aires de Securite Ed. 4: Guide des Aires de Securite Ed. 4
Gut gepflegtes Feld. Bäume am Anfang des: A well-maintained field. Trees at the western end of the field. It is possible to fly over the roof of the farmhouse, which is lower down.
Gut landbar, Naturwiese: Easy to land on, natural meadow
Gut landbar, ganze Fläche frisch gemäht: Easy to land on; the whole area has been freshly mown
Gut, Gras noch tief: Well, the grass is still thick
Gute Landemöglichkeit wenn nix mehr geht: A good landing option if all else fails. Only French or English may be spoken on the airfield frequency 123.50. The authorisation now explicitly allows us up to 15 take-offs per day, with the possibility of a short-term increase to up to 20 take-offs for meteorological reasons, subject to subsequent compensation. Previously, only a maximum of 15 ‘flight movements’ were permitted. We will therefore, for the foreseeable future, never accept more than 13–14 aircraft at any one time at our airfield. This will hopefully ensure that the friendly, informal and peaceful flying environment at Sainte-Croix is maintained for a long time to come. For formal reasons, the permit is held by the French club, ‘Aero-Club du Lac de Sainte Croix du Verdon’, which for this reason works in close cooperation with our Aero-Club Provence e.V. One inconvenient condition of our licence is the requirement to register all pilots and aircraft using this private airfield. We must submit this registration annually at the start of the season, in the spring. Anyone who is not registered is not permitted to fly at the airfield! Contact: Aero-Club Provence e.V. -- Karl Heidler, Heideweg 108, 49477 Ibbenbüren. ---------------------------------. Tel: 0 54 51 - 562 88 88. Email: karl@heidler-ibb.de.
Gute Möglichkeit falls es knapp nicht au: A good alternative if you can’t quite make it to Thun Aerodrome. Valley winds usually blow from the north-west.
Gute Option, Wildwiese leicht uneben abe: A good option; the wild meadow is slightly uneven but landable. The only option for Runway 09, as the terrain rises steeply to the east. The meadow lies on a small plateau, so the touchdown point is difficult to judge! Cattle are likely to be grazing there!
Guter Zustand vor und hinter der Ausspül: In good condition both before and after the drainage channel. The centre line is clearly marked. It’s easy to ride past it. The grass is currently very long.
Gutes Feld, recht lang. In beide Richtun: A good field, quite long. Accessible from both directions.
Gutes Landefeld. Auf einer angrenzenden: A good landing site. There is a boundary post in an adjacent meadow to the north-east.
Habe das Feld im Sommer 2022 besucht, da: I visited the field in the summer of 2022, and the power line was still there then. This year, I drove past the field and didn’t notice the power line any more. In the current French off-road catalogue (Guide des aires de sécurité, 4.1), the power line is no longer listed, unlike in version 3.
Habe die Wiese südöstlich von der Stroml: I chose the meadow south-east of the power line. The meadow becomes very undulating towards the west, towards the power line. [ WeGlide flight ]
Hallo zusammen. Vielen Dank an alle Spen: Hello everyone. A big thank you to all the donors!!!
Hallo,. Die Oberfläche von Montgardin is: Hello. The surface at Montgardin is listed as an asphalt track in the file. This is incorrect; the surface is actually grass. Best regards! Tore Graeber
Hallo,. Ich war letztes Jahr vor Ort und: Hello. I was there last year and saw both the old and the new ones. The old one is indeed a disaster, but the new one (the one in your catalogue) is now a landing site for paragliders, complete with a windsock and two large open areas. I walked around it and took a few photos as well. I’d rate it at least a B, if not an A, although I’m not familiar with your criteria. It’s bound to be a bit bumpy, of course. But I wouldn’t have any qualms about landing there. I’ve got the photos on my mobile and can send them if needed. We’re also welcome to have a chat on the phone. Regards, Rene. 00393917481600
Heute besichtigen, sieht gut aus. Man kö: I’m going to have a look today – it looks good. You could add: - that the meadow slopes gently upwards. - that along the road there are not only streetlights but also a power line running at a height of 5 metres. - you can also land perfectly by the road if you fly over the power line. As it slopes gently upwards, the distance is easily enough. I’d actually prefer that.
Heute durfte ich das Feld benutzen. Zum: I was allowed to use the field today. Fortunately, the farmer had just cut the vegetation, so that wasn’t a problem. Because of the wind, I approached the field from the west. It went well, but it’s definitely not to be recommended. On the approach, there’s a row of trees and two high-voltage power lines on the left.
Heute mit der Drohne in der nähe gewesen: I was out in the area with the drone today and took a few photos to show that the landing pad is still in good condition. It’s still very easy to land on and in good condition.
Hier handelt es sich tatsächlich um eine: This is actually a lake, and you can only land there when it’s frozen over in winter!
Hier liefere ich noch ein Bild aus einem: Here’s another still from a video showing one of the two wooden posts we saw in/by the northern field.
Hier noch 2 Bilder zur Meteostation, auf: Here are two more photos of the weather station, taken on 2 May 2002
Hier noch ein Bild von der nördliche Tal: Here’s another photo of the northern side of the valley, a little way above the landing field
Hier noch ein zweites Foto der vorher be: Here is a second photo of the pasture fences described earlier, running right through the middle of the field.
High voltage line on the east side: High voltage line on the east side
High-voltage power line on the east side. We’ve documented a few meadows as a club.
I agree, use this area in the south-east: I agree, use this area in the south-east, not the north-west part. In 2020 we only found one access for a trailer (N 43.7543 E 6.4533 at the west corner of the field at the little house) which already seemed difficult. 360°-view here:.
