I'm myself a glider pilot (bruno, discus: I'm myself a glider pilot (bruno, discus b WL D-4756) and I live in the area. The field reported in your database is no longer maintained, and should be replaced by the new field about 500m to the East. Landing eastward (typical, in the afternoon, with the valley breeze blowing from the East) the presence of the light powerline (height, 7/8 meters) running along the paved road leading to the village of Barco should be taken in due account. The new field is very visible from the air (see pictures attached) and reference can be made to a pair of very tall, prominent trees immediately NW of the field. Landing in direction West is done in calm air, or with substantial Northerly winds that tend to turn from the West down in the valley. Since the field is at least 500m long, landing in direction East, it's better to glide clearing the powerline from above, and touch beyond the trace of trail right abeam the pair of tall trees -- again, see pictures.
ICAO LIQQ - FREQ. 130.00 Tel. (+39) 0575: ICAO LIQQ - FREQ. 130.00 Tel. (+39) 0575.653445 E-mail info@aeroclubserristori.it Aviation and automotive fuel is available. Good restaurant AND accommodation right on the airfield with pool and sporting club. Ideal location for motorgliding as no tow services are available. A real gem of an airport!.
Ich bin 58 Jahre alt und seit je her woh: I am 58 years old and have lived in Plaffeien all my life; I have been paragliding since 1986/87, flying a delta wing in 1988 and a two-seater delta wing in 1990. I also hold a fixed-wing pilot’s licence and this year I passed my solo flight proficiency test on a glider. From the perspective of an ASK 21 pilot, I consider this field to be a good option. For the most part, it can be used along its entire length, as I have marked it. Even though they are separate fields, they combine to form a huge, long area. And I wouldn’t describe the meadow as slightly sloping, but rather as flat. Of course, I’ll leave it up to you whether you’d prefer to classify the field as Category B. I’ve just got a few recent aerial photos for you. Taken on 2 September 2023. I hope this is of use to you. Kind regards, Kili
Ich bin am 23.06.2001 auf der großen Wie: On 23 June 2001, I landed on the large meadow running parallel to the main road to Garmisch-Patenkirchen. This meadow poses no problems, as it lies within the wind system, offers a clear approach and is long enough. The other meadows are also suitable for landing, but some offer a restricted approach due to numerous haystacks and scattered fences. The long meadow is definitely the first choice, and its prominent location directly parallel to the road also makes it the easiest to find.
Ich bin am 24.5.03 im Rahmen des AAC 200: I landed here on 24 May 2003 as part of the AAC 2003. The area around Brunneck offers several landing options, including a model aeroplane airfield and, I believe, a disused airfield, although I have not yet been able to find any further information about it. The field where I landed is long enough and presents no problems on approach; according to a former glider pilot from the area, it has been used on several occasions and is situated directly on a main road on the outskirts of Brunneck. Landing direction approx. 300°, towards the road/town entrance. As the maize was still young, this field might not be usable in summer. To the left and right of the field were meadows which, as far as I could see, had no ditches or fences. Perhaps someone from the region has more information on other landing options?
Ich bin nicht am Boden dort gewesen, hab: I haven’t been on the ground there, but I did take a few photos from the air at about 800 metres above ground level. The landing area looks good, but the fields next to it are also suitable for landing in spring. Sorry about the reflections in the canopy. Best wishes, Conny
Ich habe den Platz mal in google earth m: I had a look at the site on Google Earth. The runway there is incredibly narrow (< 20m), and on both the right and left there are obstacles such as bushes and a hall, so there’s very little room. With a wingspan of 18m +X, landing there seems impossible to me. At 15 metres, you’d certainly need a steady hand. Has a glider ever landed there before?
Ich habe die Wiese am 22. 4. 2022 angese: I inspected the field on 22 April 2022. A pile of gravel has been dumped in the north, and there is a tall barrier standing to one side in the centre. The surface is very uneven. The field is certainly still usable in a pinch (somewhere between categories B and C), but as Belluno airfield is ‘just round the corner’ (12 km away and 60 m lower), you should avoid this field.
Ich habe die Wiese(n) am 22. 4. 2022 bes: I visited the meadow(s) on 22 April 2022. As regards the former airfield, I share the others’ view. Although it was clear that day and therefore easy to land on, the risk of it being blocked seems relatively high. The Audi team were also active, though only in the very northern part, which wouldn’t be a problem. However, a little further south-east (almost right next to it) there is something that looks like a former sports ground. When approaching from the north, you still have to fly over some flagpoles, but after that the field is relatively flat. Land in the rear section on the right-hand side (there’s a ditch on the left). My suggestion: if you consider both options (the former airfield and the former sports ground), this would be a Category B site.
Ich habe mir am Osterwochenende 2012 die: I went to have a look at the fields in Andermatt over the Easter weekend in 2012. The longest field (north of the road running alongside the railway line) is bordered to the west by a farmstead. On this farmstead stands a grey mobile phone mast 20–25 metres high – which is probably not visible from the air. This makes an approach over this farmstead for a north-easterly landing direction dangerous or impossible.
Ich habe mir am Samstag die Wiese angese: I went to have a look at the meadow on Saturday; I wasn’t able to check the coordinates, but it seems that this spring the northern half of the field was indeed planted with apple trees and concrete posts, so that there is now a very short uphill landing area (with irrigation pipes running through it). According to my photos from 2009, it was still significantly longer and relatively free of obstacles when approaching, although there were already sprinkler pipes back then. If push came to shove, I’d still prefer to land at Plaus, provided this field is still there in 2011!!!
Ich war als Rückholer bei der Außenlandu: I was there as part of the recovery team when the first pilot made an emergency landing on this field – given the complex situation involving the obstacles on the ground, the short distance and the slope, I wouldn’t classify it as Category A, but rather somewhere between B and C. Without a prior site survey, it’s probably unlikely you’d manage a safe landing there.
Ich war im Mai 2026 vor Ort. Grundsätzli: I was there in May 2026. In principle, there are several fields here that are suitable for landing. However, the terrain rises from the north-east to the south-west. The approach should therefore be made from the north-east, diagonally across the landing field. The approach should be made roughly over the gravel pit.
Ich war im September 2023 vor Ort. Den E: I was there in September 2023. I couldn’t identify the entry ‘Schluderns 2022’ as a possible landing site amidst the tall grass and undulating ground, but I couldn’t rule it out beyond doubt either. I’m not sure what to do about it – perhaps leave it as a ‘C’? According to the description, it’s difficult to identify the correct strip, and at 180m (?) it’s really extremely short. However, I was able to confirm that the three areas marked in the uploaded image are all suitable for landing, at least with a 15–18m aircraft. I’d list all three as Cat B. I’ve made a video of each one; here are the links, followed by a brief summary for all three meadows: Schluderns South: I can confirm the previous entries as they stand. Approximately 23 metres wide, 250 metres long, with a relatively unobstructed approach from both sides. The width of the irrigation channel appears to be fixed, and the strip in between looks unchanged from years ago. The irrigation system is probably barely visible from the air.
Ich war im September 2023 vor Ort. Ich h: I visited the site in September 2023. I filmed a video of each location; here are the links, followed by a brief summary for all three meadows: Schluden’s former model aeroplane airfield: Unfortunately, the model aeroplane airfield has been closed, but the meadow still exists. Irrigation poles have been installed; the swath between them is approximately 23 metres wide and 250 metres long. Aerial irrigation is probably barely visible. This means the landing conditions are similarly good/poor to those at ‘Schluderns South’. Schluderns North: Runs parallel to the road, where the paragliding windsock is also located. A few metres to the south-east, running parallel to the road, is the first row of irrigation pipes; then there is 25 metres of space to the next row, followed by another 25 metres of space – in other words, two parallel ‘lanes’. Irrigation systems are likely to be barely visible from the air. Heading towards 04, a dirt track appears after 180 metres; however, it is flat and should be easy to drive over at low speed. The actual end of the track only comes after 280 metres in the form of a bump.
Ich war letztes Jahr (mit dem Auto) in M: I went to Merano last year (by car) and had a close look at the trotting track there. It was cordoned off, so I could only view it from the outside, but what I saw was far from impressive. In my view, the track is so narrow – and, above all, bordered by a tall hedge – that, with a lot of luck, a 13-metre glider might just manage to land there. I consider it dangerous to recommend this track for 15-metre gliders that aren’t absolute high-wingers! Given the importance of this area, I believe it is imperative to warn against entering this option into the system without first inspecting the site in person.
Im April 2005 steht eine Baumaschine dra: In April 2005, a construction machine is standing on it, laying pipes in numerous trenches.
Im April 2022 besichtigt; mE weiter eine: Visited in April 2022; in my view, still a good option
Im September 21 in der Mitte der Landest: On 21 September, in the middle of the country route, an obstacle spanning the entire width of the AUDI Erlebniswelt
Im südwestlichen Viertel steht hohes Get: In the south-western quarter, the grain is tall; otherwise, the land is easily accessible.
Im östlichen Teil der Wiese (ab dem Weg): Excavation work is taking place in the eastern part of the meadow (from the path onwards). The western part remains available (450m).
Im östlichen Teil der Wiese wurden mehre: Several houses have been built in the eastern part of the meadow!
Immer noch eine hervorragende Landewiese: It’s still an excellent landing area, although the grass is a bit long at the moment. You just need to open and hold the barriers for the retriever; these are located on the access road. The first one is a little to the south of the field on the farm track, and the second one is, if you follow the farm track westwards, right by the small village of Fall.
In Tabland sind die Stangen dichter beie: In Tabland, the poles are closer together than indicated in the SiP. Furthermore, the landing area is several hundred metres higher than indicated in the SiP.
In einem engen Tal. Anspruchsvoller Anfl: In a narrow valley. A challenging approach.
In the approach from east high voltage l: In the approach from east high voltage line!
Ist ganz hervorragend! Von Ost nach West: It’s absolutely brilliant! It rises from east to west. A Piper is stationed there.
Ist nicht empfohlen auf diesen Feldern z: It is not recommended to land on these fields as the wind is usually blowing at right angles to the direction of flight.
Ist zwar ein UL Flugplatz aber die Wiese: It is a UL airfield, but the meadows in the valley are much better. Site visit and photo from 2015; aerial view from 2019 remains unchanged
Je nach Bepflanzug ins Feld (ca. 220 m l: Depending on the vegetation, land in the field (approx. 220 m long), otherwise in the meadow (approx. 180 m long and narrower). Both areas slope downwards from the road. If possible, land diagonally!
Just a reassuring update on the conditio: Just a reassuring update on the conditions of the field, that I visited and walked on Feb. 03 2025 -- I'm a glider pilot (Bruno from AVUT trento, D-4756) and I live in the area. The field is perfect, as previously described. Here you find a picture that I shot from the road immediately West of the field. The two tall, prominent trees that may serve as a reference from the air are circled in green.
Kabelverseuchtes Tal. Seilbahn auf Germs: A valley criss-crossed with cables. Cable car to Germsstock on the eastern approach. Several landing options; be aware of narrow fields, vegetation boundaries and a ditch (stream) in the southern field. Prefer the Altdorf and Seedorf landing fields (30 km, 1,000 m lower).
Kat. B, da laut Samedaner Flieger gibt e: Category B, as according to Samedaner Flieger, there are frequent breakages there!
Kat. B: Problem: enges V-Tal, Ramosch. A: Cat. B: Problem: narrow V-shaped valley, Ramosch. Halfway up, an aircraft carrier-style landing!
Kein Container, keine Hochspannungsleitu: No containers, no high-voltage power lines – so the field should be easy to land on. When landing heading south, try to touch down as centrally as possible just before you reach the tree, as that’s where the undulations in the ground are least pronounced. During my visit, three paragliders landed on the meadow. There is a windsock by the road, but it is partially obscured by the surrounding trees.
Kein hoher Bewuchs, siehe Bilder. Die Gr: No tall vegetation, see photos. The ditches/furrows are as already described by others.
Keine wesentliche Änderung. Aber das Fel: No significant changes. However, the terrain is very undulating, particularly in the eastern part. Where possible, land in the western part, towards the village of Klosters. On 18 April 2022, an off-field landing was carried out successfully without any damage to equipment.
Keine wesentliche Änderung. Das Feld ist: No significant change. The field is still well suited for this purpose, despite the motorway service station built in the northern part near the motorway exit. The same applies to the western field situated above the embankment.
Keine wesentliche Änderung. Sehr kurzes: No significant changes. Very small area. Bordered on both sides by buildings (west side) and fruit trees (east side)
Pebble track 06/24 (not grass!). Length now 250 m (not 350 m). Pile of stones at the start of track 24: usable length 200 m.
Pebble track 06/24 (not grass!). Length now 250 m (not 350 m). Pile of stones at the start of track 24: usable length 200 m.
Kunstwiese. Anflug nur aus Osten entlang: Kunstwiese. Approach from the east only, following the power lines. Watch out for the two street lighting columns (Schachenstrasse) on the approach! (These are 42 metres apart)
Kurzer UL Landeplatz im Durancetal. Nur: A short UL landing site in the Duran Valley. For landing only; the site is not suitable for take-off as it is too short.
Kurzer UL-Platz. Video mit beiden Landea: A short UL airfield. Video showing both approach manoeuvres:
LS 7 FI ist hier bei starker Bise geland: LS 7 FI has landed here in strong Bise winds.
La Mure wurde zum UL Flugplatz angepasst: La Mure has been converted into a microlight airfield and is therefore maintained on an ongoing basis. The actual landing strip runs downhill to the line of the dirt track, 250 m in length, or to the level of the tree where the microlight is parked. The former hill has been levelled and can be overrun. This gives a total length of 400 m in an emergency. The width is also fine; it might be a bit narrower with open-class gliders, but landings are still possible. Pascal offers a very warm welcome and assistance; he can be reached on 130.00. He also runs a gîte, so overnight accommodation might be possible in an emergency. La Mure operates on the mountain flight frequency 130.00 MHz, so it’s worth tuning in to this frequency in the event of a possible out-of-field landing. There is active flight traffic from local microlights (sometimes up to 15 maiden flights a day).
Landbar auf 03/21, wegen Buckeln besser: Landable on 03/21; due to ridges, it is better to stay on a north-westerly course (towards the river). The windsock suggests a paragliding landing site. 03 is preferred due to the rising terrain.
Lande-T am Pistenanfang: Lande-T at the start of the runway
Landeanflug 23 frei, Feld ansteigend (da: Approach 23 clear, field rising (so a landing in the opposite direction is not recommended!). Some thermals! It’s better to head more towards the centre of the valley. Another option is to go one step further towards the centre of the valley.
Landed on 07.05.2025. Good ultra light f: Landed on 07.05.2025. Good ultra light field, no obstacles, but a reasonable slope torward the east direction. We landed to the east, would not recommend landing to the west unless it is very strong wind. [ WeGlide Flug ]
Landed there in july, field is in good c: Landed there in july, field is in good condition, owners are friendly and helpful
Landed there on 21-05-2026. Surface is g: Landed there on 21-05-2026. Surface is good, grass is kept trimmed by. Horses. Beware of the horses, they wander around and get curious after landing. Yell "Frau Blücher!" to keep them away. There are significantly high trees in the east side. The owner is in the house in the west side. [ WeGlide Flug ]
Landen zwischen der Strasse und der Sche: A field between the road and the barn with the red roof. The breeze usually comes from the east.
Landerichting ist sicher nicht 09/27 son: The runway direction is certainly not 09/27 but 18/36
Landerichtung ausschließlich NW. Modella: Landing direction: north-west only. The model aircraft catch net extends into the field and limits its width at the narrowest point to approximately 30 metres. Flat valley floor. Contact the model flying club:
Landewiese Anfang Jänner 2026 besichtigt: The landing field was inspected in early January 2026; no visible obstacles, plenty of space. It is better to approach from south to north (landing direction 35), as the field slopes gently upwards towards the north.
Landing 20 pay attention to the street l: Landing 20 pay attention to the street lamps 10/12 meters high with an interdistance of about 30 meters. Landing 02 pay attention to the ditch that crosses the field.
Landing direction north-west just at the: Landing direction north-west just at the beginning (ditch at the end). (In 2020 also the field right (east) of the marked strip was ok, but might vary with vegetation.) Maybe turbulences with north-west-wind. Approach with car/trailer rather from north-east than south east (bad road). 360°-view here:. Photos Jörn Leiber in August 2020.
Landing to the south over obstacles; tre: Landing to the south over obstacles; trees, power lines. Real landing distance only 150m. Better to use the fields 3km to the south.
Landung am 09.06.2025. Felder unveränder: Landing on 9 June 2025. Fields still in very good condition. [ WeGlide flight ]
Landung am 12.06.2021. Wiese unverändert: Landed on 12 June 2021. The meadow remains in very good condition. Flight:
Landung am 17.08.2025. Die beste Möglich: Landing on 17 August 2025. The best option is to land diagonally from north-east to south-west, as this allows you to land slightly uphill. Alternatively, you can land from east to west or vice versa, or from north to south (i.e. up the slope). However, make sure to keep to the right. [ WeGlide flight ]
Landung am 19.07.2022. Sehr gute Wiese,: Landing on 19 July 2022. Very good meadow; the northern part (along the tarmac track/cycle path) is very flat, with only a slight rise in the southern half. Hardly any gradient, so very easy to land in both directions. Windsock on the solitary building in the north-west corner of the meadow. A few wooden posts mark the boundaries, though these are mainly in the southern part. Overall, a very good option.
Landung am 24.06.2023 (ausnahmsweise mal: Landing on 24 June 2023 (for once, not by me, but by a fellow flight instructor). The meadow was still in very good condition. [ WeGlide flight ]
Landung am 30.03.2023. Weizenfeld. Solan: Landing on 30 March 2023. Wheat field. As long as the crop is short or has been harvested, the field is definitely category A; the boundary is not clearly visible during the approach, but it is easy to fly over. Unfortunately, the farmer has had very bad experiences in the past with glider pilots who, after making off-field landings on this field, simply disappeared without taking responsibility for the damage caused to the crop. He therefore requests that the following notice be published here in three languages: PLEASE DO NOT LAND HERE – LANDOWNER STRONGLY DISAPPROVES OF OUTLANDINGS. PLEASE DO NOT LAND HERE – LANDOWNER STRONGLY DISAPPROVES OF OUTLANDINGS. DÉFENCE D’ATTERRISSAGE DANS CE CHAMPS – PROPRIÉTAIRE N’ACCEPTE PLUS D’ATTERRISSAGES
Landing on 30 July 2024. Still going strong. [ WeGlide flight ]
Landung im August 2020 auf 18/36 50M Bre: Landing in August 2020 on runway 18/36, 50 metres wide and 500 metres long, in a cornfield
Landed in March 2024. In very good condition. The surface looked as though it had been worked by a farmer; it is level and hard enough to roll on easily.
Landung und Wiederstart am 18.06.2025 mi: Landing and take-off on 18 June 2025 with a Duo Discus. The airfield is perfectly flat, long, with an unobstructed approach, and has a valley wind blowing in parallel to the runway. Definitely Category A. On thermal days with no dominant long-range wind, there is a strong valley wind from the north-north-west. The runway was perfectly mown very short, but there was tall grass to the left and right of the runway. Although there is no tow plane on site, F-tow is possible if a tow plane can be organised from Grenoble Le Versoud, Challes-les-Eaux or, as in our case, from Barcelonette. Laut Website www.altiportdecorlier.fr (A: According to the website www.altiportdecorlier.fr (accessed on 8 December 2021): Altiport, steeply rising towards the south-east, therefore only approach direction 12 is possible; turbulence in southerly winds; frequency 130,000. Information sourced solely from the internet; not personally verified.
Length: Northern field 500 m (watch out: Length: Northern field 500 m (watch out for wooden piles), southern field 380 m. Might be wet in spring. 360°-view here:. Photos Jörn Leiber in August 2020, taken from the curve of the route at the northeast corner of the southern field (N 43.782, E 6.740).
Liebe Streckenflug.at, am letzten Mittwo: Dear Streckenflug.at, last Wednesday I landed on the field in Dubino. The landing direction of the field is approximately 33, i.e. 90° to the one currently shown on the website. The approach began at a height of about 200 m, with the Delebio church tower visible, roughly 2 km south-east of the field. The landing spot was actually a narrow (about 25 m) strip of dark brown, fertilised soil in the middle of the field, clearly visible from Delebio. The meadow itself conceals hazards, such as slightly rough ground beneath the grass and a few wooden stakes driven into the ground. From above, the meadow shown on the website and the 04/22 landing direction did not strike me as feasible.

Manchmal sind alle vier kleinen Felder z: Sometimes all four small fields are joined together to form one large field. However, the two middle fields are more than enough. High-voltage power line to the south
Mehrere Felder mit unterschiedlicher Bep: Several fields with different crops. Photo taken facing south on 15 April 2022
Mehrere Felder sind landbar, nach Bewuch: Several fields are suitable for landing; choose one based on the vegetation.
Mehrere große Äcker, je nach Bewuchs wäh: Several large fields; choose according to the vegetation.
Mehrere gute Felder. Geeignetes auswähle: There are several suitable fields. Choose the most appropriate one, depending on the vegetation or livestock. Watch out for the high-voltage power line running from SSW to NNE!
Mehrere parallele Streifen in Nord-Süd-R: Several parallel strips running north-south. Field boundaries clearly visible due to differences in colour. Fences and ditches in places along the field boundaries. Landing direction is south to north only. Valley wind (on thermal days with no wider-area wind) from the north-east. The landing strip shown here allows for a completely unobstructed approach from the south over a high embankment (aircraft carrier-style landing). The houses at the short end of the strip lie well below the level of the meadows and therefore do not constitute an obstacle.
Mein Anflug 2025 von Nordosten von der S: My approach in 2025 from the north-east, coming from the town (unlike the diagram). A power line on the south-eastern side of the valley is difficult to see. A low group of trees and tall floodlights from the adjacent sports ground; flying through between them is perfectly feasible. The meadow slopes down towards the river on the western side. Unlike the latest satellite image on Google, the western part is not a field of oats, but a meadow that is not too damp. The approach is relatively narrow, over the neighbouring property to the north-east. The farmer feels as though he’s been dispossessed and needs plenty of reassurance. [ sis-at flight ]
Mitte Juni waren beide Felder noch nicht: By mid-June, neither field had been mown yet. Local pilots say there is a possibility that the farmers might leave the silage bales lying in the meadow, which would then make it unsuitable for landing. But it is generally a good landing field.
Mittlerweile fix installierte Bewässerun: Irrigation pipes are now permanently installed in the field. Cannot be landed on.
Modellflug südlich; südlich der Ahr stei: Model aircraft flying south; south of the Ahr, the terrain rises
Cereals currently growing in the field.
Momentan ein Maisfeld. Besichtigung am 7: Currently a maize field. Visit on 7 July 25

Muss besichtigt werden. Bahnlinie auf We: A must-see. Railway line on the west side.
Muss besichtigt werden. Steigt gegen NW: A must-see. Climbs towards the north-west
Nach Schließung des Modellflugplatzes Sc: Following the closure of the Schluderns model aeroplane airfield, we have found another (emergency) landing site further south. Unlike almost all other fields throughout the Vintschgau valley, this one is not criss-crossed with irrigation poles or used for fruit cultivation; instead, it is an open field with just enough length, but only wide enough for small aircraft. Irrigation poles on the left and right edges of the ‘runway’, spaced approximately 22–24 metres apart. Clear in between. Length just under 250 metres. Unobstructed approach from the south-east; trees in the north-west approx. 100 metres before the threshold, with maize in front of them. Site visit on 16 August 2021; information and images: Markus Full. Google Street View from 2011 is available. At that time, it was still arable land, with no grass.
It can still be landed as stated.

Neu ist ein UL-Platz, 100 m über dem Eis: There’s a new UL airfield, 100 m above the Eisack River! It’s 300 m long and shaped like a bathtub (hollow)!!??? There are 22-metre-wide gaps between the thresholds! Difficult approach! In front of the northern threshold, the terrain drops vertically for 100 metres! Two wind socks are in place!
Neu: Massive Betonblöcke über die ganze: New: Solid concrete blocks running the full width divide the meadow into two parts. In addition, approximately 20 m south of the line of concrete blocks, there is a raised steel manhole cover in the centre, which may not be visible if the grass is tall (see photo: view northwards from the manhole cover). It is still possible to land in both directions: touch down after the concrete blocks, then roll out for approx. 210 m. When landing from the south, do not touch down in the centre, but land in the western or eastern section to avoid the manhole cover. The eastern section was in better condition. The problem with the meadow is that it is used as a playing field and sports ground; even during the site visit on 30 December 2025, there were people on the meadow.
Nicht gemähte/ungepflegte Wiese, somit k: Unmown/unmaintained meadow; it is therefore not possible to assess its condition in the tall grass. The access road and entry to the landing area are closed.
Niedriger Bewuchs am 27.06.2023. Bewässe: Low vegetation on 27 June 2023. But the watering drum is already waiting by the side.
Low vegetation, looks good (27 June 2023). I haven’t actually walked into the field myself, though, so please check for yourself if you’re in any doubt.
No free approach, fields across landing: No free approach, fields across landing direction, prefer Lauerzersee!
Normale Platzrunde nicht möglich. Aufset: A standard circuit is not possible. Touch down immediately after the dirt track. The final 20 metres of the meadow are steeply uphill; there is no significant incline before that. Successful outfield landing on 22 March with an ASW-28/18m. Alternatives: directly north of the Möhlinbach, west of Wegenstetten; three fields; approach direction 06/24 depending on the field.
Nur Richtung 030° landen da Hochspannung: Only when heading towards 030° do high-voltage power lines run to the north of the field
Nur Richtung 340° anfliegbar. Gelände st: Can only be approached from 340°. The terrain rises towards the north-west. Railway line to the south. From the Dittingen 2008 catalogue.
Nur aus der Luft zufällig entdeckt. Sche: I only spotted it by chance from the air. It looks like a private airfield.
Nur von Osten her anfliegbar. Achtung be: Can only be approached from the east. Caution: in Bise or easterly winds, strong rotors form behind the edge of the woodland --> approach from a high altitude.
Nur von Westen her anfliegbar hangaufwär: Can only be approached from the west, flying up the slope. A DG300 has already landed here.
Nutzung durch Pferdehof intensiviert, Fe: Use by the horse farm has increased; fields further subdivided by fences! No reliable landing area – used as a paddock. Landing in short sections may still be possible.
Nördliche Wiese gut landbar, Hochspannun: The northern meadow is easy to land on; there is a high-voltage power line to the west of the runway
Oberfläche: Wiesen - leicht wellig. Stem: Terrain: Meadows – slightly undulating. Ridges in the western section and smaller ones along the central meadow path. Choose a landing zone based on how the land is used. The best conditions are found parallel to the central meadow path, to the west and east. Landing possible on both sides; no power lines.
Observe vegetation borders, some rollabl: Observe vegetation borders, some rollable, western approach sports facility with light poles
Often better than Gattinara airfield whe: Often better than Gattinara airfield when very low
Piste volle Länge in Betrieb und in sehr: The runway is fully operational and in very good condition. Grass is growing onto the taxiway, but it is still easy for motorised aircraft to taxi on it. It is best to taxi off the runway only at the far south end, in the middle (near the hangars) and at the far north end. No air traffic controller. Only submit blind reports. The fuel station is not in operation. Infrastructure is in place, but in poor condition (e.g. toilets). There is a club with a number of powered aircraft, as well as the region’s rescue helicopter, based at the airfield.
Poorly visible cable car in the south of: Poorly visible cable car in the south of the fields
Private airstrip, good surface, grass us: Private airstrip, good surface, grass usually kept mowed. The strip is narrow but there are no obstacles on the sides, maybe some not-too-high crops. Elevated terrain all around but plenty of space for approach. The strip may be difficult to spot until right overhead. Access closed by a gate but the friendly owner or neighbors do help opening it.
Private grass strip mainly for ultraligh: Private grass strip mainly for ultralight traffic. Field manager: Gilberto Capparella - Tel. 366-6068217. Data from:.
Recherche im Internet! Keine Besichtigun: Research online! No on-site visit! The microlight airfield looks good according to the 2023 Google Maps map. Grass (400 x 20).
Recht Uneben. Wenige Holzpfosten in der: Quite uneven. A few wooden posts in the meadow
Relativ steiler Anflug nötig, da die Bäu: A relatively steep approach is required, as the trees along the approach are quite tall compared to the length of the meadow. Landings at 11 and 29 are equally feasible (please add further details). The surrounding meadows are not well suited due to uneven ground and overhead cables.
Rhäzüns kann auf beiden Seiten des Hügel: You can land in Rhäzüns on either side of the hill.
Riesige Wiese, mitten drauf ein Modellfl: A huge meadow, with a model aeroplane airfield right in the middle of it. A circus comes to town once a year, but even then there’s still plenty of space to land.
Riesiges Feld, ca. 400m lang und durchau: A huge field, about 400 metres long, and perfectly well suitable for landing from the 04 direction. Only slightly uphill. It is only the easternmost part that slopes more steeply. Trees and power lines on the approach are not a problem, given the size of the field.
Schwieriges Landefeld wegen fester Bewäs: A tricky landing area due to fixed irrigation poles! A strategically important meadow if you don’t want to land at the racecourse in Merano! Approach from south to north over a farm! Then you’ve got a chance of hitting a 30-metre-wide corridor of irrigation poles running parallel to the eastern footpath! Kite and paraglider landing site! Years ago, Fridolin saw a plane landing there from the air! The aircraft still looked intact!
Sehr fragwürdige Notlandemöglichkeit, di: A highly questionable emergency landing option, which must under no circumstances be taken into account, but which is still listed in various landing guides – presumably partly because the Meran Model Sports Team also uses this field from time to time: And there has in fact already been at least one successful emergency landing by a motorised aircraft on 12 May 2008 (see the BFU Bulletin 2008 under reference number SX002-0/08 and a newspaper report). Nevertheless, anyone who has the opportunity should go and assess the conditions for themselves on site. Apart from the fact that people and animals may well be present here during events – and indeed at other times too – it is uncertain whether a new obstacle course might not make landing impossible. Furthermore, any landing would have to be a high-precision manoeuvre with a high risk of damage; a wingspan of more than 15 metres would probably not be feasible! The field has therefore been classified as unsuitable for landing! In general: the area west of the Jauffen Pass, stretching into the Vintschgau valley as far as Schluderns, offers no landing options; this region must therefore be flown over with due caution! Otherwise, the Bolzano airfield to the south should always be planned as a landing site, whilst also taking into account the strong valley wind system from the south!
Sehr lange gute Landewiese. Bodenbeschaf: A very long, good landing area. The ground is level and firm. A few 1-metre-high posts for birds of prey.
Seit mehreren Jahren befindet sich ca. I: For several years now, there has been a weather station situated roughly in the middle of the landing area. It is clearly visible and does not cause any significant obstruction. Landings can still be made on both the left and right-hand sides of the weather station.
Servus,. Die Fotos die jetzt im cupx lan: Hello. The photos currently on cupx are out of date. The newly built farm in the middle of the old meadow isn’t in them. The more recent photos are by me and Manfred Schwegler.
Several fields across the processing lin: Several fields across the processing line or transitions of the individual fields!

Siehe Sat-Bild mit Skizze: Die Wiese lie: See satellite image with sketch: The meadow lies to the west, running alongside a row of trees. Running roughly parallel to the row of trees, at a distance of approximately 17 metres, is a row of irrigation pipes (red dots). To the west of this, there is an open strip approximately 40 metres wide leading up to the next row of sprinklers. Due to a pronounced dip in the landing area, you should aim to land as far south as possible in both directions, i.e. close to the farm.
Small airfield but in very good conditio: Small airfield but in very good condition.
Sollte in Ostrichtung angeflogen werden,: You should approach from the east, as the terrain rises to the east!
Sorry, I forgot to add that there is a n: Sorry, I forgot to add that there is a noticeable slope thus the recommended landing direction is 11

Stand 29.03.2024:. Feld gut Landbar, die: As at 29 March 2024: The field is easy to land on; the high-voltage power line is no longer there. However, the field remains quite uneven due to bumps, small shallow ditches and dips in the ground. When approaching from the north, it is best to land in the centre of the field, heading south across the village, with a slight turn over the road to avoid any turbulence from the slope on the right if necessary.
Stand 29.04.2024:. Wie durch Jan im Vide: As of 29 April 2024: As Jan mentioned in the video, the field is a good place to land. It’s best to land diagonally past the row of bushes in the middle, either towards the joinery/village or towards the woods. Both sides are approx. 200–300 metres long. The side of the field right next to the road is dangerous due to turbulence.
Steinhaufen Ende 24 derzeit sehr klein,: The stone pile at the end of Runway 24 is currently very small, with the landing area elevated by less than 10 metres. The runway slopes gently downwards. In my estimation, it is possible to touch down (approaching from Runway 24) approximately 20 metres after the start of the runway. At the end of runway 24, the grass is currently short (which should allow for a smooth touchdown without damage from the gravel).
Steinpiste 06/24. Vorsicht Länge 250 m j: Stony track 06/24. Caution: length 250 m at this point; pile of stones at the start of track 24: usable length 200 m.
Stoppelige eher schlecht gepflegte Grasp: A stubbly, rather poorly maintained grass runway belonging to the local microlight club; 20 metres wide between markers that are lower than most wings. See photo.
Strommasten auf der Nord-, Süd- und Osts: Electricity pylons on the north, south and east sides
Suedwestlichste Wiese sehr buckelig, Hol: The south-westernmost meadow is very bumpy, with wooden stakes in it. There is a better alternative further east.
Swiss Grid baut dort Strommasten für ein: Swiss Grid is assembling electricity pylons there for a new high-voltage power line and piling up the old pylons in a heap. Part of Field 2 is therefore blocked off, and helicopter flights are also to be expected! Field 1 is now being irrigated; you can tell by the hose reel. The farmer keeps moving the sprinkler and the hose reel around to water the newly cultivated field. Please note: a thick hose is then lying somewhere between the sprinkler and the reel and the connection point at the corner of Field 1. Thanks to Max Lamm from Samedan for the latest information!
Südlich der Mitte in ca 3-4m Höhe ein dü: To the south of the centre, at a height of about 3–4 metres, a thin wire is suspended from two flimsy wooden slats.
Südlich des Flusses 350m lange Wiese. Nö: South of the river, there is a 350-metre-long meadow. To the north of this, there is also potentially a 190-metre-long meadow, which is flatter.
Südliches Feld:. Beim Anflug von Süden h: Southern field: When approaching from the south, a high power line crosses the landing field. Northern field: OK.
Take care about rolls of grass in the mi: Take care about rolls of grass in the middle - make it unusable.
Teile der Fläche sind bepflanzt (Mais, G: Parts of the field are under cultivation (maize, cereals). However, there is a strip of land suitable for landing.
The correct name is \"Calcinate\", not \: The correct name is "Calcinate", not "Calcinate Piemon". The site is gliding airport. The runway grass, 10/28 is 600x50 m. Standard circuit for gliders is left hand runway 10. Frequency 128.455.
Topfeben. Gross genug. Möglichst an der: Completely flat. Big enough. If possible, land on the south side of the meadow, heading east or west. The road running alongside the meadow is called Flugplatzstr, or something like that. Perhaps you could get away from here by microlight tow?
Touch down in the second half after the: Touch down in the second half after the path!
Trabrennbahn. Die nur mehr zeitweise gen: Trotting track. The trotting track, which is now only used occasionally, is the only landing option in this valley all the way to Bolzano. According to reports, the (eastern?) narrow side – the track itself – is about 20 metres wide and suitable for landing; inside, there are irregularly spaced obstacles that may vary over time. The approach (preferably from the south-west?) may be obstructed by lighting masts. As there are conflicting reports on this, this option is probably only really suitable for 15-metre aircraft; the risk of damage increases with wingspan, and it is likely to be completely unsuitable for the Open Class.
Two high overhead power lines cross the: Two high overhead power lines cross the field and need caution. A red/yellow pole is placed in the middle of the road. Field is in good condition. There is a worn out windsock close to the road entrance. Keys to open the gates may be obtained from the Continental camping nearby.
UL Feld gut zugänglich. Vegetation links: UL field easily accessible. Vegetation to the left and right of the runway strip is in places tall (maize). Width only approx. 25 m,
UL arifield, Approach only 22m wide, run: UL arifield, Approach only 22m wide, runway very narrow and short! Cornfield alternatives are only until june landable!
UL runway. Very narrow, flanked by trees. The runway curves slightly in an S-shape. Only suitable for gliders with a wingspan of 15 metres or less. Land uphill! (from south to north)
Um mit dem Auto an die Wiese ranzukommen: To get to the meadow by car, you need a key. You can probably get one from one of the houses on the access road.
Unmittelbar nach der Landung sollte der: Immediately after landing, the glider pilot should try to contact one of the following three landowners: a) Sebastian Regli 079 602 70 02 b) Hans Regli 079 231 03 45 c) Wendelin Regli 041 887 19 75
Unverändert schöne Außenlandewiese: A landing field that remains as beautiful as ever
Unverändert, Bewässerungsanschluss in de: Unchanged; the irrigation connection in the middle of the field is marked with a red post, though this is not a factor when driving between the electricity pylons.
Unverändert, gute Landemöglichkeiten. Hi: Unchanged, good landing options. Note: Christian Holzner’s comment dated 15 October 2003 is unclear. Does it perhaps refer to a different landing site???
Unverändert, viele Bodenwellen, sehr sch: Unchanged, lots of bumps, a very difficult course.
Unverändert. Mehrere Landemöglichkeiten,: Unchanged. Several landing options, including outside the marked areas
Unverändert. Schön flaches Feld mit nur: Unchanged. A nice, flat field with very few bumps, but a slightly more difficult approach. But generally well suited. Note: Uwe Hartmann’s comment dated 24 February 2004 is unclear; it may refer to a different field.
Unverändert. Stromleitung und Luftseilba: No change. Watch out for the power line and the Feldis cable car when approaching from the south
Unverändert. Wegen der Baumreihen auf be: Unchanged. Due to the rows of trees on both sides of the field, the length actually available is limited. This does not generally cause any problems, but it may be necessary to fly low over it during the final approach.

Von dem UL-Platz wurde abgeraten. Altern: The UL site is not recommended. An alternative is on the other side of the river (meadows and fields; flat and without a gradient). Please note: some areas have (mobile?) irrigation systems. The landing strip must be chosen depending on how the land is currently being used.
Von der Hauptstrasse gegen Süden seitlic: Slightly sloping away from the main road to the south. The two farm tracks are passable by vehicle. The main field to the west is 230 metres long
Vorsicht, ich hab mir die Wiese öfters a: Be careful – I’ve had a good look at the field a few times, and the approach area is incredibly tight, even for a 15-metre aeroplane. I don’t think I’d be able to land a Duo there without a bit of a struggle. As far as I’m concerned, the field is only really suitable for small aircraft (similar to the Schluderns model airfield) – and even then, it’s certainly not a walk in the park.
War gut, wie im Katalog beschrieben. Zug: It was good, just as described in the catalogue. Access by car is from the rear, through a farmstead, not from the main road. There is also a (private) barrier. [ WeGlide flight ]
Watch out for Walkers! Approach in a cur: Watch out for Walkers! Approach in a curve over the lake.

Wenn es irgendwie geht, immer die Wiese: If at all possible, always choose the meadow in Obervintl over any other options in the Brixen area! Please note: there is a high metal fence at the crossroads! Power lines to the south and east! The meadow slopes gently down towards the south and leans slightly towards the west. There are relatively tall trees in the approach.
Westlicher Teil in der Nordhälfte beweid: The western part of the northern half is grazed. The southern half is a newly sown artificial grassland and is suitable for landing. The eastern half has vegetation approximately 20 cm high.
Westliches Feld ist frisch gemäht und gu: The western field has been freshly mown and is easy to land on. The northern field still has tall crops.
Westliches Feld: kann gut aus beiden Ric: Western field: can be approached easily from both directions. Northern field: Caution – high-voltage power line runs alongside the railway line
Wie beschrieben unverändert gute (beste): As described, the conditions in Entrevaux remain good (the best). Vegetation at the end of August 24 was uneven, 20–30 cm high. Small trees and shrubs are beginning to appear. Valley winds prevail in thermal conditions from the east.
Wiese (1):. Die Wiese liegt direkt nord-: Meadow (1): The meadow lies directly north-east of the valley station of the large ‘Col Rodella’ cable car, which runs north-north-west. A striking feature is a high-voltage pylon consisting mainly of concrete pylons. The landing is made from south-west to north-east, approaching along the valley axis, passing east of the valley station and east of the prominent high-voltage pylons. Watch out for tall trees and a green safety net (2 m high) whilst still on approach. Touch down late! From the valley station, the concrete-pylon power line runs in a straight line north-east towards Canazei. The landing area begins to the east of this line, next to an iron lattice mast, hugging the eastern slope! There is also a windsock here! Ahead of this, the first 100 metres of the meadow are undulating and a small transverse ridge hinders a smooth landing (now a golf driving range)! After that, the terrain improves, though it becomes increasingly narrow! To the right (west) of the power line, where the windsock is also situated, the ground is more undulating, but it would still be possible to land right next to the high-voltage line! It is only further north – that is, at the end of the field – that several 50-cm-high iron posts, positioned across the direction of approach, appear at intervals of about 20 metres!
Wiese (2):. Diese Wiese liegt ca. 600 m: Meadow (2): This meadow lies approximately 600 m north-east of Meadow (1), in the direction of Canazei. It, too, lies along the route of the concrete-pylon power line, but to the north-west of it. It is approximately 300 m long. The south-western threshold is formed by two barns situated 50 paces apart. Land to the west, near the footpath, as a embankment borders the meadow to the east. The cross-wind approach, which runs diagonally to the valley axis, should begin above the Col Rodella valley station and lead over the western row of trees (approx. 10 m high) that border the meadow area. The final section is unfortunately very short, but feasible.
Wiese entsprechend den Bildern vorgefund: The meadow was found to be as shown in the pictures.
Wiese ist etwas uneben nur über die Stra: The meadow is slightly uneven and can only be approached from the lake, across the road. It slopes gently upwards towards the east. There is a small ditch running across the meadow after about 400 metres. At that point, you should keep to the south, where the ditch is barely noticeable.
Wiese unverändert gut!: The meadow is still looking good!
Wiese weiterhin sehr gur landbar: The meadow remains very suitable for grazing
Wiesen werden teilweise durch mobile Hüh: Some of the meadows are blocked by mobile chicken coops.
Wiesen, Leitung am Feldweg entlang der L: Meadows, a path running alongside the field track by the landing field, updated based on Google Street View
Wir haben nur die nördlichen Landefelder: We’ve only seen the northern landing fields. They’re currently easy to land on, though part of the field is bare (perhaps to be built on in future?)
Wir sind auf dem Feld zwischen den beide: We ended up in the field between the two marked options (between the road and the river), as the vegetation looked better there. It’s a bit shorter, and the motorway during the short final approach means you need to fly in at a slightly higher angle. But it was no problem at all.
Während meiner Besichtigung Mitte August: During my visit in mid-August, there were lots of bales of straw on both meadows – please note! Even without these, the northern meadow is still a good option.
You can roll over the crossing path!: You can roll over the crossing path!
You can roll over the crossing path, spo: You can roll over the crossing path, sports facility with light poles in the western approach.
You can roll over the crossing paths: You can roll over the crossing paths
You didn't like my advice? See on the ma: You didn't like my advice? See on the map with satelite picture: It's corn that is planted!!! But who cares, isn't it?;-)
Zu empfehlen! Am besten von Osten anflie: Highly recommended! It’s best to approach from the east. A power line crosses the eastern section. The northern boundary is marked by a high-voltage power line.
Zur Karte: Zwischen der Verengung im nör: About the map: Land south of the crossroads, between the narrow section in the northern part (where a farm track cuts across the meadow) and the road in the southern part. Regarding the photos taken from the ground: the first photo was taken in the direction of 340° (exactly opposite to the landing direction); from the house visible in the centre-right of the photo, the farm track runs leftwards across the meadow; the second photo was taken at 180° and shows the southern boundary of the field.
Zusatzhinweis: Bei Mäharbeiten werden wo: Additional note: When mowing, metal parts are often torn out and left sticking up. These can damage the tyres or even the chassis. It may therefore be a good idea to land next to the metal plates – most likely to the south of them.
Zwei aktuelle Bilder vom 31.07.2024. Bli: Two recent photos from 31 July 2024. View towards the west. The field to the left of the path is the officially designated one, but the one to the right of the path is currently better, as the vegetation there is not as tall or dense. The harvested field behind it should also be fine (the second photo shows the transition between the two fields). Access from La Batie is via a dirt track, which has slightly larger stones on the steep section. A small car with a large trailer might not cope very well here. Further up, the track is in good condition. In the little village there’s a small Buddhist centre where, after landing in the open countryside, you can meditate and reflect on your flying decisions ;-)
Zwischen Fluss (Birs) und Eisenbahn/Land: Between the river (Birs) and the railway line/country road. The railway line is electrified (wires/pylons). From the Dittingen catalogue, 2008.
Zäune und Querbewirtschaftung beachten,: Take care to avoid fences and cross-country farming; land upstream; approach over the Rhine
bei der Besichtigung am 15. Juni war die: During the site visit on 15 June, the meadow hadn’t been mown yet; a few days later, I saw from the air that the meadows had been mown. It is possible that silage bales are lying in the meadows after mowing, which would make landing more difficult. [ WeGlide flight ]
bin vor einigen Tagen dort vorbeigefahre: I drove past there a few days ago. The field can no longer be used as building work is taking place on it.

- 23 September 2024, Jan Lyczywek: Inspection on 2 September 2024. The meadow remains in very good condition. The owner and the tenant are noticeably and understandably annoyed by the numerous paraglider landings (and presumably even landings by microlights?!). During our conversation, we tried to explain the difference between such landings ‘for fun’ and our emergency landings, which, of course, nobody does for fun. The owner urged us to include a note here stating that she and her tenant do not wish to have any landings on their property. She also asks that people refrain from frequent visits to her property, as it has apparently already happened that cars have turned round in the tall grass or even driven into the meadow to have a look around! - 29 March 2024, Felix Maier: As of 29 April 2024: As already mentioned by Jan in the video, the field is easy to land on. It is best to land diagonally past the row of bushes in the middle, either towards the joinery/village or towards the forest. Both sides are approx. 200–300 metres long. The side of the field directly next to the road is dangerous due to turbulence.
- 25 November 2021, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 4 September 2021. The meadow is flat and suitable for landing in both directions. There is a ditch running along the western row of bushes; between the end of the row of bushes in the west and the windsock in the east, there is a 50-metre-wide gap. There is no ditch there, but an underground drainage pipe; it is uneven but can be driven over. For both landing directions, it is better to treat the drainage pipe as a threshold and touch down just beyond it. Valley wind (on days with consistent thermals and no penetrating cross-regional wind) from the south. Site inspection video:

During my visit, three paragliders landed on the meadow. There is a windsock by the road, but it is partially obscured by the surrounding trees.

However, the field remains quite uneven due to undulations, small shallow ditches and dips in the ground. When approaching from the north, it is best to land in the centre of the field, heading south over the village, with a slight turn over the road to avoid any turbulence from the slope on the right.
- 24 October 2023, Stefan Hässig: I visited the field in the summer of 2022, when the power line was still there. This year, I drove past the field and didn’t notice the power line any more. The power line is no longer listed in the current French cross-country flying catalogue (Guide des aires de sécurité, 4.1), unlike in version 3.

en\u001fInfo: Former airfield, partly us:
Info: Former airfield, partly used as a motorhome site; pedestrians; helicopter landing pad in the centre.
Surface: Asphalt runway
Direction: 17/35
Visited: 2022
Updated: 24 April 2022
Feedback:
- 24 April 2022, Erik Kurtz: I visited the field(s) on 22 April 2022. Regarding the former airfield, I share the others’ opinion. Although it was clear on that day and therefore suitable for landing, the risk of it being blocked seems relatively high. The Audi team were also active, though only in the very northern part, which wouldn’t be a problem. However, a little further south-east (almost right next to it) there is something that looks like a former sports ground. When approaching from the north, you still have to fly over some flagpoles, but after that the field is relatively flat. Land in the rear section on the right-hand side (there’s a ditch on the left). My suggestion: if you consider both options (the former airfield and the former sports ground), this would be a Category B site.
- 30 October 2021, Dr Arno Hutter: In September 2021, an obstacle spanning the entire width of the landing strip in the middle of the runway caused by AUDI Erlebniswelt
- 21 February 2021, Christoph Dolainsky: Partly used as a motorhome parking area; pedestrians on the old runway! Helicopter landing pad in the middle.
en\u001fInfo: Golf course with 2m net in:
Info: Golf course with a 2m net on the approach, partly very uneven
Surface: Grassland
Direction: 09/-
Inspected: 2013
Updated: 10 April 2013
Reported hazards: uneven ground, power lines/other cables
Feedback:
- 14 March 2009, Ulrich Plarre: Meadow (1): The meadow lies directly north-east of the valley station of the large ‘Col Rodella’ cable car, which runs north-north-west. A prominent feature is a high-voltage power line consisting mainly of concrete pylons. The landing is from south-west to north-east, with an approach along the valley axis, passing east of the valley station and east of the prominent high-voltage power line. Watch out for tall trees and the green safety netting (2 m high) whilst still on approach. Touch down late! From the valley station, the concrete-pylon power line runs in a straight line north-east towards Canazei. The landing area begins to the east of this line, next to an iron lattice mast, hugging the eastern slope! There is also a windsock here! Ahead of this, the first 100 metres of the meadow are undulating and a small transverse ridge hinders a smooth landing (now a golf driving range)! After that, the terrain improves, though it becomes increasingly narrow! To the right (west) of the power line, where the windsock is also located, the ground is more undulating, but it would still be possible to land right next to the high-voltage line! It is only further north – i.e. at the end of the field – that several 50 cm high iron posts, positioned across the direction of landing, appear at intervals of approx. 20 m!
- 14 March 2009, Ulrich Plarre: Meadow (2):. This meadow lies approximately 600 m north-east of Meadow (1) in the direction of Canazei. It, too, lies along the continuation of the concrete-pylon power line, though to the north-west of it. It is approximately 300 m long. The south-western threshold is formed by two barns 50 paces apart. Land to the west, near the footpath, as a embankment borders the meadow to the east. The cross-wind approach, which runs diagonally to the valley axis, should begin above the Col Rodella valley station and lead over the western row of trees (approx. 10 m high) that border the meadow area. The final section is unfortunately very short, but feasible.
- 14 March 2009, Ulrich Plarre: The part of the meadow used by paragliders is very undulating and not recommended.

en\u001fInfo: LANDING NOT POSSIBLE ANY M:
Info: LANDING NO LONGER POSSIBLE!
Surface: Grassland
Direction: 14/32
Inspected: 2023
Updated: 1 May 2024
Feedback:
- 15 December 2023, Benjamin Bachmaier: I visited the site in September 2023. I took a video of each location; here are the links, followed by a brief summary for all three meadows: Schluden’s former model aeroplane airfield: Unfortunately, the model aeroplane airfield has been closed, but the meadow still exists. Irrigation poles have been installed; the swath between them is approximately 23 metres wide and 250 metres long. Aerial irrigation is likely to be barely visible. This means the landing conditions are similarly good/poor to those at ‘Schluderns South’. Schluderns North: Runs parallel to the road, where the paragliding windsock is also located. A few metres to the south-east, running parallel to the road, is the first row of irrigation pipes; then there is 25 metres of space until the next row of irrigation pipes, followed by another 25 metres of space – in other words, two parallel ‘lanes’. Irrigation from the air is likely to be barely visible. Heading towards 04, a dirt track appears after 180 metres; however, it is level and should be easy to drive over at low speed. The actual end only comes after 280 metres in the form of a bump in the ground.
- 9 August 2023, Hans Trautenberg: Webcam showing the old airfield, as well as the section where festivals are occasionally held.
- 15 March 2021, Jakob Edmaier: Landing direction exclusively NW. The model catch net extends into the field and limits the width at its narrowest point to approx. 30 m. Flat valley floor. Contact model flying club:
- 19 November 2008, CVAO: initial feedback

This field is listed in the French off-field landing catalogue “Guide des aires de sécurité” as off-field landing site No. 312 and is an alternative to the nearby “Valenty” cereal field. Generally speaking, the wide Durance Valley offers a great many flat areas in this region; however, some of these are unavailable all year round due to fruit cultivation, whilst others are unavailable depending on the season due to cereal cultivation. Unfortunately, it is unclear what is being grown on the field shown here. At the end of March, it looked like grass. Site visit video:

- 26 April 2022, Rudolf Schneider: Inspection on 23 April 2022: The irrigation system is currently in place. The steel poles are approximately 4 cm thick and 90 cm high. You should be able to see them during the final approach to land within the clear strips. Once the system has been dismantled, we have a first-class landing field 350 m long, level and completely smooth. See the satellite image below for the sprinkler layout. There is a wall running alongside the road, and the first row of sprinklers (red dots) runs parallel to it, approximately 2 m away. There are then clear strips, each approximately 31 m wide, leading up to the next row of sprinklers.
- 15 March 2021, Roland Henz: Actually a good meadow and one of the few heading south towards the Jauffen Pass. The irrigation systems were also a problem during the site visit on 24 September 2016. The distance would be suitable for at least 15 m and 18 m, but would probably not be visible if the vegetation were taller. The irrigation pipes are also visible on Street View!

- 2 May 2022, Rudolf Schneider: Smooth meadow with no sprinkler system. Running across slightly south of the centre, at a height of approx. 3–4 m, a thin wire (telephone?) is suspended from two flimsy wooden slats. See satellite image with sketch. Landing from the south, approaching over the trees, is fairly straightforward. Short landing! Touch down before the wire! Landing from the north, approaching over Quellenhof with a very steep descent, is quite difficult if you want to touch down before the wire. In an emergency, the wire is likely to give way.

en\u001fInfo: Very narrow and short! Obe:
Info: Very narrow and short!
Surface: Field
Direction: 15/33
Inspected: 2024
Updated: 1 May 2024
Comments:
- 1 May 2024, Benjamin Bachmaier: I visited the site in September 2023. Given the tall grass and undulating ground, I couldn’t definitively identify the entry ‘Schluderns 2022’ as a possible landing site, but I couldn’t rule it out beyond doubt either. I’m not sure what to do about it – perhaps leave it as a ‘C’? According to the description, it’s difficult to identify the correct strip, and at 180m (?) it’s really extremely short. However, I was able to confirm that the three areas marked in the uploaded image are each suitable for landing, at least with a 15–18-metre aircraft. I would list all three as Category B. I took a video of each one; here are the links, followed by a brief summary for all three meadows: Schluderns South: I can confirm the previous entries. Approximately 23 metres wide, 250 metres long, with a relatively unobstructed approach from both sides. The width of the irrigation channel appears to be fixed, and the path between them has looked unchanged for years. The irrigation is probably barely visible from the air.
- 5 September 2021, Benjamin Bachmaier: Following the closure of the Schluderns model aeroplane airfield, we have found another (emergency) landing site further south. Unlike almost all other fields throughout the Vintschgau, this one is not criss-crossed with irrigation poles or used for fruit cultivation, but is an open field with just enough length, though only wide enough for small aircraft. Irrigation poles on the left and right edges of the “runway”, spaced approx. 22–24 metres apart. Clear in between. Length just under 250 metres. Unobstructed approach from the south-east; trees in the north-west approx. 100 metres before the threshold, with maize in front of them. Site visit on 16 August 2021; information and images: Markus Full. Google Street View from 2011 is available. At that time, it was still arable land, with no grass.

en\u001fInfo: horse racing track, a lot:
Info: horse racing track, numerous barriers, approx. 20m wide
Surface: Horse Race Track
Direction: 04/-
Visited: 2009
Updated: 27 March 2009
Reported hazards: uneven ground, obstacles on the ground
Feedback:
- 15 March 2021, Roland Henz: A highly questionable emergency landing site which must under no circumstances be relied upon, yet is still listed in various landing guides – presumably also because the Merano Model Sports Team occasionally uses this field: And there has in fact already been at least one successful emergency landing by a powered aircraft on 12 May 2008 (see the BFU Bulletin 2008 under reference number SX002-0/08 and a newspaper report). Nevertheless, anyone who has the opportunity should go and assess the conditions for themselves on site. Apart from the fact that people and animals may well be present here during events – and indeed at other times too – it is uncertain whether a new obstacle course might not make landing impossible. Furthermore, any landing would have to be a high-precision manoeuvre with a high risk of damage; a wingspan of more than 15 metres would probably not be feasible! The field has therefore been classified as unsuitable for landing! Generally speaking: the area west of the Jauffen Pass, stretching into the Vintschgau valley as far as Schluderns, offers no landing options; this region must therefore be flown over with due caution! Otherwise, the Bolzano airfield to the south should always be planned as the landing site, whilst also taking into account the strong valley wind system from the south!
- 27 March 2009, CVAO: initial feedback
- 25 March 2005, Thomas Bauer: Completely flat. Large enough. Land on the southern side of the meadow, heading east or west if possible. The road by the meadow is called Flugplatzstr, I think. Perhaps you could get away from here via a microlight tow?
- 9 February 2004, Sepp Holzapfel: I was in Merano last year (by car) and took a close look at the trotting track there. It was cordoned off, so I could only inspect it from the outside, but what I saw there was absolutely not impressive. In my opinion, the track is so narrow – and, above all, bordered by a high hedge – that, with a lot of luck, a 13-metre ‘Spatz’ might just manage to land there. I consider it dangerous to recommend this track for 15-metre gliders that aren’t high-wing designs! Given the importance of this area, I believe it’s imperative to warn against entering this option into the computer as a possibility without first inspecting it in person.

Feedback:
- 2 April 2023, Urs Wolfensberger: The satellite image only shows the (preferred) route with an approach across the valley. Whilst the description is generally correct, I would suggest specifying an approach towards 31 (so as not to contradict Figures 5 and 6). When approaching from direction 31, the meadow does indeed lie to the left, but only then is the stated field length of 250m realistic; from direction 36, it is at most 170m. The considerable gradient of approx. 6% should also be noted. The entry dated 7 March 2021 is out of date. These residential buildings are workers’ accommodation from the former Gotthard Tunnel construction site, which was dismantled long ago. These are no longer visible on the satellite image used by streckenflug.at, nor in Ernst Will’s approach video dated 5 April 2020. I would delete Image 1 (as it also contradicts Image 4) and replace Images 5 and 6 with images that do not show the demolished houses (see also the appendix). For images 1 to 4, it would be helpful to include a note indicating the direction in which the photograph was taken – towards 09 or 27 (i.e. perpendicular to the 31 approach).
- 7 March 2021, Christian Hynek: Several residential buildings have been constructed in the eastern part of the meadow!
- 27 March 2011, Christian (DD): initial feedback
en\u001fInfo: watch out for posts and fe:

Surface: Grassland
Direction: 15/33
Inspected: 2023
Updated: 25 April 2023
Reported hazards: Ground obstacles, power lines/other cables
Feedback:
- 25 April 2023, Marco Maceri: Dear Streckenflug.at, last Wednesday I landed on the meadow in Dubino. The landing direction of the meadow is approximately 33, i.e. 90° to the one currently shown on the website. The approach began at around 200 m, using the church tower in Delebio as a reference point, approximately 2 km south-east of the meadow. The landing spot was actually a narrow (about 25 m) strip of dark brown, fertilised ground in the middle of the meadow, clearly visible from Delebio. The meadow itself conceals hazards, such as slightly rough ground beneath the grass and a few wooden stakes driven into the ground. From above, I did not notice the meadow shown on the website or the landing direction 04/22 as being possible.
- 10 April 2013, Christian (DD): initial feedback

- 25 April 2022, Rudolf Schneider: Depending on the vegetation, land in the field (approx. 220 m long), otherwise in the meadow (approx. 180 m long and narrower). Both areas slope away from the road. If possible, land diagonally!
- 27 June 2011, Hans L. Trautenberg: There is a meadow suitable for landing at a heading of 50°, 173 metres long. The path at the end cannot be driven over in the section where it runs at a heading of 110°. Approach between the trees and touch down immediately after them. Aim closer to the track, as there is an irrigation system further away from it. Approach between the hill and the church. The field, which runs in a 50° direction, can be approached from a 230° direction.

en\u001fN Reco a pied indispensable. Att: A visual approach is essential. Land facing north.
en\u001fN/S Champ difficile. Fosses buse: N/S Difficult field. Drainage ditches in the middle of the field. Approach over the first field and land on the second. A walk-through reconnaissance is essential.
en\u001fN/S Reco a pied indispensable. P: N/S: On-foot reconnaissance essential. Mobile fencing may be required. Meadows.
en\u001fN/S Remonte vers le N. Attention: N/S Climbing towards the north. Take care in northerly winds (turbulence). Other fields may be used.
en\u001fN/S Remonte vers le N. Turbulenc: N/S Moving northwards. Turbulence from the north-west. Other fields may be possible.
en\u001fN/S Tres delicat. Reco a pied in: N/S Very tricky. A guide is essential. Lots of potholes.

Feedback:
- 16 February 2021, Robert Zinnecker: The runway is fully operational and in very good condition. Grass is encroaching on the taxiway, but it is still easy for powered aircraft to taxi on. It is best to taxi off the runway only at the far south end, in the middle (near the hangars) and at the far north end. No airfield manager. Only submit blind reports. The fuel station is not in operation. Infrastructure is in place, but in poor condition (e.g. toilets). There is a club with a few powered aircraft, as well as the region’s rescue helicopter, based at the airfield.

- 20 May 2025, Rene Fischer: Here are two additional photos. The field is significantly longer than the stated 250 m. Paragliders land on the section marked in red.
- 7 August 2024, Rene Fischer: Hello. I was there last year and saw both the old and the new sites. The old one is indeed a disaster, but the new one (featured in your catalogue) is now a landing site for paragliders, complete with a windsock and two large open areas. I walked around it and took a few photos as well. I’d rate it at least a B, if not an A, although I’m not familiar with your standards. It’s bound to be a bit bumpy, of course. But I wouldn’t have any qualms about landing there. I’ve got the photos on my mobile and can send them if needed. We’re also welcome to have a chat on the phone. Regards, Rene. 00393917481600

- 19 March 2023, Jan Lyczywek: photos from the video
- 19 March 2023, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 14 March 2023. The fields near Montmaur are listed as Field No. 311 on page 136 in the official outfield landing catalogue ‘Guide des aires de sécurité dans les Alpes, Édition 3’. Two areas are marked there; the field described here, “Montmaur Nord”, is the northern and slightly poorer of these two areas. It slopes upwards from south to north, so landing from south to north is preferable. Take care when flying low over the river and road from the south: a low-hanging power line with concealed pylons crosses the southern, pointed end of the meadow in a roughly west-east direction. Site inspection videos: Part 1: Part 2:

- 9 June 2025, Jan Lyczywek: Landed on 9 June 2025. Fields still in very good condition. [ WeGlide flight ]
- 14 March 2023, Jan Lyczywek: Inspection on 14 March 2023. Excellent fields. Slightly ascending from south to north. Landing in calm conditions therefore from south to north. When there is a southerly wind, it is also possible to land from north to south. The very longest field lies directly to the east, parallel to the road, but the longest of the fields to the west of the road is also very good. Do not drive over the field boundaries (=colour boundaries). Irrigation systems are laid underground, but the individual sprinklers do not appear to be permanently in place; in any case, there were none there in mid-March. Site visit video:

- 9 September 2023, Kilian Raemy: I am 58 years old and have lived in Plaffeien all my life; I have been paragliding since 1986/87, flying a delta in 1988 and a two-seater delta in 1990. I also hold a fixed-wing pilot’s licence and this year I passed my solo flight proficiency test on a glider. From the perspective of an ASK 21 pilot, I consider this field to be a good option. It can usually be used along its entire length, as I have marked it. Even though they are separate fields, they combine to form a huge, long area. And I wouldn’t describe the meadow as slightly sloping, but rather as flat. Of course, I’ll leave it up to you whether you’d prefer to classify the field as Category B. I’ve just provided a few recent aerial photos, taken on 2 September 2023. I hope this is of use to you. Kind regards, Kili
- 6 January 2022, Bernhard Bärfuss: Due to the buildings in Plaffeien, it’s better to approach from direction 05. Otherwise, there are few suitable outlanding fields in this area.

Feedback:
- 2 July 2024, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 25 June 2024. Approach possible from both directions. Minimal cross-slope from the road in the north down to the river in the south. The power line running alongside the road does not cause any obstruction. The field is quite stony; apart from that, it is Category A. Previously, a transverse field boundary was visible on Google Maps, dividing the area at its narrowest point into two roughly equal sections. This field boundary was definitely no longer present during the on-site inspection on 25 June 2024; the field has clearly been tilled and sown in a single continuous stretch along its full length. Should this remain the case in the long term, a very important new landing site has been created, making the route to Mont Ventoux significantly safer! Site inspection video:

Surface: Field
Direction: 09/27
Inspection: 2025
Amended: 5 June 2025
Feedback:
- 4 June 2025, Jan Lyczywek: Inspected on 2 June 2025. Unchanged factors supporting a Category A classification: - Unchanged shape of the field. - More than adequate length. - Very level ground. - No gradient whatsoever. - Sufficient unobstructed approach from east to west. - Approach is therefore against the valley breeze blowing from the west. A new, but unfortunately only temporary, factor in favour of Category A: - at the moment (early June 2025), the field is covered in recently harvested grain! Great! A factor in favour of Category B: - It is unclear what will be grown there next. Will it be ploughed up? Will maize be planted again? Site inspection video:
- 27 June 2023, Thomas Reinhold: Currently (27 June 2023), there is only very low vegetation in the western part between the path and the row of trees, offering a good opportunity. However, this could change quickly!
- 25 March 2023, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit on 24 March 2023. Field unchanged. In spring, as long as the maize hasn’t grown tall yet and the irrigation pipes haven’t been laid, this is a very good, Grade A field. Later in the year, however, it will be inaccessible due to the maize and irrigation pipes – see Christian Heiting’s note! New site visit video:
- 30 March 2022, Jan Lyczywek: Site visit by friends on 29 March 2022. A good field now in March whilst the vegetation is low. However, the irrigation pipes were already in place. High vegetation possible in summer! Site inspection video: The field near Espinasses is listed as Field No. 317 on pages 142–143 in the French out-of-bounds catalogue “Guide des aires de sécurité dans les Alpes, Édition 3”.
